PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120

[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/) Solar Energy Program (SEP)
# Solar Energy Program (SEP)
  * [Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/solar-energy-program-guidelines/?wpdmdl=67690)
  * [Contact Us](https://dced.pa.gov/contact-us)
  * [](javascript:window.print\(\) "Print")


###### Overview
The Solar Energy Program (SEP) provides financial assistance in the form of grant and loan funds to promote the use of solar energy in Pennsylvania. The program is administered jointly by the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) and the Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) under the direction of the Commonwealth Financing Authority (CFA).
###### Uses
Activities to promote the generation and use of solar energy and the manufacture or assembly of solar equipment.
###### Eligibility
  * Businesses
  * Economic development organizations
  * Political subdivisions, including municipalities, counties and school districts


###### Funding
Grants
Grants for component manufacturers of solar energy generation equipment up to $5,000 for every new job created within three years after approval of the loan. Grants for solar energy generation or distribution projects shall not exceed $1 million or $1.50 per watt, whichever is less. In exchange for a grant, Solar PV projects will contractually commit to transfer ownership of the Solar Renewable Energy Credits (SRECs) generated by the system over to the CFA for the life of the project.
Loans
Loans for component manufacturers of solar energy generation equipment up to $40,000 for every new job created within three years after approval of the loan. Loans for solar energy generation or distribution projects shall not exceed $5 million or $3.00 per watt, whichever is less.
Loan Guarantees
Loan guarantee grants shall not exceed $5 million and have a term of not more than five years. In the event of a default, the grant will pay up to 75 percent of the deficiency.
###### Terms
There is a matching investment requirement of at least $1 for every $1 of program grant funds awarded. There is a matching investment requirement of at least $1 for every $3 of program loan funds awarded. There is a $100 non-refundable application fee due at the time of submission made payable to the CFA. There is a 1% commitment fee on all approved loans.
### Additional Information
[Solar Energy Program Fact Sheet](https://dced.pa.gov/download/solar-energy-program-fact-sheet/?wpdmdl=121594)
### Program Details
#### Program Categories
  * Grant
  * Loan
  * Loan Guarantee


#### Funding Types
  * Business
  * Community
  * Local Governments

# Programs & Funding
## If you’re seeking funding for a business expansion, a community project or site revitalization in Pennsylvania, DCED offers a variety of programs to help.
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") Programs & Funding
![Programs & Funding](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/resources-money.svg)

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Small Business Assistance
## Pennsylvania Business One-Stop Shop is the go-to resource for planning, registering, operating, and growing a business
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") Small Business Assistance
Entrepreneurs, startups, and small businesses bring new ideas, tools, technology, and jobs to Pennsylvania, creating opportunities throughout our state. They employ about half of the state’s workforce, and in second half of 2015, created nearly 24,000 new jobs.
To support our entrepreneurs and businesses at all stages of development, the Pennsylvania Business One-Stop Shop was created and launched [business.pa.gov](https://business.pa.gov/) in February 2018.
Serving as the first point of contact for Pennsylvania business development concerns, the Pennsylvania Business One-Stop Shop connects and guides businesses through all stages of development — from planning and startup to operating and expanding.
[![PA Business One-stop Shop](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/BusinessOneStopShop-Hori_2025.png)](https://business.pa.gov/)
##### The Entrepreneur’s Guide
[![Entrepreneurs Guide](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2016/11/E-GuideCover2016_150.jpg)](https://dced.pa.gov/download/entrepreneurs-guide/?wpdmdl=56163)
Download PA’s comprehensive business resource guide to help you launch your business in Pennsylvania. Here you’ll find expert information regarding building a business plan, registration, funding advice, and more. Our e-guide is printer-friendly and eco-friendly!
##### Veterans Resources
[![Veteran Resources Factsheet](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/Veterans-Resources_Factsheet_2025.png)](https://dced.pa.gov/download/veterans-resources-fact-sheet-2018/?wpdmdl=83145)
Download the Veterans Resources Fact Sheet and learn how you can start or grow your business in Pennsylvania. From technical and registration help, to securing funding for a diverse business, our team can help.
[Contact Form](https://paiedprod.powerappsportals.us/one-stop-shop-contact/)
### [Download Brochure](https://dced.pa.gov/download/pa-business-one-stop-shop-brochure/?wpdmdl=72707 "PA Business One-Stop Shop Brochure") [ ![PA Business One-Stop Shop Brochure](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/BizOneStopShop_Brochure_2025_Page_1.jpg) ](https://dced.pa.gov/download/pa-business-one-stop-shop-brochure/?wpdmdl=72707 "PA Business One-Stop Shop Brochure") [](https://dced.pa.gov/pa-business-one-stop-shop-brochure/?wpdmdl=72707 "Download or View the PA Business One-Stop Shop Brochure")
Interested in doing business in Pennsylvania?
[Contact Us](https://dced.pa.gov/contact-us) [Available Sites](http://www.pasitesearch.com/)
![Payback Logo](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/svg/PAyback-logo.svg)
Eligible for a refund of your permits, licenses, or certification application fees?
[Check Now](https://payback.pa.gov/)

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Pennsylvania Community Development Bank (PCD Bank)
## The purpose of the Pennsylvania Community Development Bank is to provide grant funding and liquidity financing for State Accredited Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFIs)
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") [Business Financing](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/business-financing/ "Business Financing") [Authorities](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/business-financing/authorities/ "Authorities") Pennsylvania Community Development Bank (PCD Bank)
The Pennsylvania Economic Development Financing Authority (PEDFA), through an operational Committee of the Board, administers the Pennsylvania Community Development Bank (PCD Bank). PCD Bank meets certain requirements under the Federal Community Development Banking and Financial Institutions Act of 1994. Authorized by PEDFA, the PCD Bank’s purpose is to enhance the organizational capacity of the Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFIs), allowing them to expand their operations or offer improved services resulting in increased lending activities.
##### Operational Committee Members

[](javascript:%20void\(0\) "Share Page on Facebook")[](javascript:%20void\(0\) "Share Page on X")[](javascript:%20void\(0\) "Share Page on LinkedIn")
> For purposes of the potential bond issuance for the PlanCon project, the Commonwealth Financing Authority is represented by and will rely on the municipal advisor, Public Financial Management, Inc. (PFM), specifically including but not limited to Kathy Clupper to provide advice in evaluating recommendations or advice from financial services firms concerning the issuance of municipal securities and municipal financial products (including investments of bond proceeds and escrow investments) as such terms are defined in the SEC's Municipal Advisor Rule. This certificate may be relied upon until July 1, 2019.”

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Business Opportunities Fund
## Designed to assist businesses that lack access to traditional sources of financing
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") [Center for Private Financing](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/private-financing/ "Center for Private Financing") Business Opportunities Fund
The [Business Opportunity Fund](http://community.newpa.com/programs/business-opportunities-fund-bof/) is a partnership between the Pennsylvania Community Development Bank, the Community First Fund, Bridgeway Capital and the Pennsylvania Department of General Services. The purpose of this pilot program is to assist small minority or women-owned contractors and other small business owners who lack access to lines of credit or small business loans from traditional financial institutions since this lack of access to credit prevents these businesses from competing for governmental and private sector contracts. These business owners often require technical assistance in governmental procurement including achieving necessary certification and bid preparation. The BOF is designed to assist borrowers with both financing and technical assistance.

# Investment Tracker
## Track Investments Grants and Loans with the DCED Investment Tracker
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs & Funding](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-funding/ "Programs & Funding") Investment Tracker
Get information on executed contracts for a grant, loan or tax credit filed through DCED with our [Investment Tracker](https://apps.grants.pa.gov/investmenttracker/).

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Qualified Opportunity Zones
## On December 22, 2017, the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA) created [Qualified Opportunity Zones](https://www.enterprisecommunity.org/policy-and-advocacy/policy-priorities/opportunity-zones-program), a tool for promoting long-term investment in low-income communities.
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs & Funding](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-funding/ "Programs & Funding") [Federal Funding Opportunities](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-funding/federal-funding-opportunities/ "Federal Funding Opportunities") Qualified Opportunity Zones
The federal tax bill passed at the end of December 2017 enables the governor to designate certain census tracts as Opportunity Zones. Investments made by individuals through special funds in these zones would be allowed to defer or eliminate federal taxes on capital gains. The governor was given the opportunity to designate up to 25 percent of census tracts that either have poverty rates of at least 20 percent or median family incomes of no more than 80 percent of statewide or metropolitan area family income. There are nearly 1,200 eligible census tracts and the [governor designated 300 tracts](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-funding/federal-funding-opportunities/qualified-opportunity-zones/) based on economic data, recommendations from local partners, and the likelihood of private-sector investment in those tracts.
##### Collection of Recommendations
DCED received robust feedback following the posting of the Opportunity Zones web page.
Comments and recommendations were received from a total of 132 individuals, cities, counties, legislators, and organizations. A total of 734 eligible low-income census tracts were recommended to the state for designation, which is 61% of the entire eligible pool.
##### Tracts were Recommended Based Primarily on the Following
Potential to Eliminate Poverty
Special consideration was given to some of the poorest areas of the state:
  * 120 tracts or 40% above 35% poverty rate 
  * 198 or 66% of tracts have a median family income lower than the average of all eligible tracts
  * 202 or 67% of tracts have unemployment rate higher than 10%


Areas with Business Activity
Priorities of local economic development partners and community organizations were given special consideration as well as positive responses to the following:
  * Does the area have an anchor (airport, hospital, large employer)?
  * What is the likelihood of private sector-driven low- to moderate-income or affordable housing being developed in the tract?
  * Will future development in the tract build on previous and ongoing public investments in infrastructure?


Geographic Diversity
Consideration was given to population centers throughout Pennsylvania which have potential for investment.
  

##### Designated tracts
On June 14, 2018 , 300 tracts were designated as Opportunity Zones.
  * [List of tracts by county](https://dced.pa.gov/download/final-foz-spreadsheet/?wpdmdl=84233)
  * [Interactive map of the designated tracts](http://dced.maps.arcgis.com/home/webmap/viewer.html?webmap=b0bd4d703ddc498fb0a993a00d77ed4c)


##### Timeline for Qualified Opportunity Zones for 2018
  * **March 30** – Deadline for Public Recommendations
  * **April 20** – Governor’s Submission of Tracts to U.S. Department of Treasury
  * **June 14th** – Federal Approval of Designations
  * **October 2018** – Guidance Issued by U.S. Department of Treasury 
  * **January 2019** – Public Hearing on Regulations 
  * **April 2019** – Second round of guidance issued by U.S. Department of Treasury

Inquiries about Qualified Opportunity Zones may be directed to the PA Department of Community & Economic Development at the following email address:

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Compliance Resources
## Documents and Resources for Compliance
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs & Funding](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-funding/ "Programs & Funding") Compliance Resources
##### Grant Closeout Requirements
If the amount of the Contract is less than $1,000,000, the grant contract will require that an acceptable Grant Closeout Report be submitted. Refer to the links below for the appropriate Closeout Report.
If the amount of the Contract is $1,000,000 or more, with or without matching funds, a final audit of the entire contract (Project Audit) is required. The state reserves the right for state agencies or their authorized representative to perform additional audits of a financial or performance nature if deemed necessary.
For any questions about Close Out Requirements, contact the Audit and Compliance Division via e-mail ra-audits@pa.gov
##### Programs and Funding Compliance Library
  * ###### [Closeout Guidelines and Reports](https://dced.pa.gov/library/?wpdmc=closeout_guidelines_and_reports)
  * ###### [Other Information](https://dced.pa.gov/library/?wpdmc=other_information)


##### External Resources
  * [Government Auditing Standards (The Yellow Book)](http://www.gao.gov/govaud/ybk01.htm)

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Local Share Assessment Reporting
## The Pennsylvania Gaming Act requires municipalities to send information to the DCED
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs & Funding](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-funding/ "Programs & Funding") Local Share Assessment Reporting
All counties and municipalities receiving distributions of local share assessments are required by the Pennsylvania Gaming Act to submit information to the Pennsylvania Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED). DCED is required to submit an annual report to the Appropriations, Community, Economic and Recreational Development, and the Gaming Oversight Committees on all distributions of local share assessments (gaming funds) to counties and municipalities by March 31 of each year.
##### The Report Must Include:
  * Amount and use of funds received by the municipalities and counties in the prior calendar year.
  * A description of how the funds were used; either deposited in a general fund or committed to a specific use or project.


##### Access Report
Access the [online form](https://apps.dced.pa.gov/LocalShareReport/) to report your Local Share Assessment revenue and expenditures.
##### FAQ
View the [Local Share Reporting Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs).](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-and-funding/local-share-assessment-faq/)
##### Local Share Assessment Reports
  * [2024 Local Share Amounts And Use Annual Report ](https://dced.pa.gov/download/2024-local-share-amounts-and-use-annual-report/?wpdmdl=126571)
  * [2023 Local Share Amounts And Use Annual Report ](https://dced.pa.gov/download/2022-local-share-amounts-and-use-annual-report/?wpdmdl=126176)

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Technology & Innovation
## Spurring innovation & the advancement of new technologies
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") Technology & Innovation
![Technical Assistance and Access to Capital](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/cog-money.svg)
#### Assistance & Access to Capital
  * [Ben Franklin Technology Partners](http://benfranklin.org/ "Our Services")
  * [Innovation Partnership](http://innovationpartnership.net/ "Innovation Partnership ")
  * [Innovate in PA](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/technology-innovation/innovate-in-pa/ "Innovate in PA ")
  * [Manufacturing PA Initiative](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/technology-innovation/manufacturing-pa-initiative/ "Manufacturing PA Initiative")
  * [PA Life Sciences Greenhouse Initiative](https://www.pa-lsgs.org/ "PA Life Sciences Greenhouse Initiative")
  * [Partnership for Regional Economic   
Performance (PREP)](https://dced.pa.gov/prep/ "Partnership for Regional Economic Performance \(PREP\)")
  * [Appalachian Regional Commission (ARC)](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/appalachian-regional-commission/ "Appalachian Regional Commission")


![Network Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/globe2.svg)
#### BFTDA
  

  * [About](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/technology-innovation/ben-franklin-technology-development-authority-bftda/ "About the The Ben Franklin Technology Development Authority \(BFTDA\)")

  * [Legislative Reports](https://dced.pa.gov/library/?wpdmc=bftda-reports "The Ben Franklin Technology Development Authority \(BFTDA\) Legislative Reports")

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.

Harrisburg

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Authorities
## CFA, PIDA, PMBDA, PEDFA, and PCD Bank are independent authorities within the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED)
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") [Business Financing](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/business-financing/ "Business Financing") Authorities
### CFA
  * [About](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-funding/commonwealth-financing-authority-cfa/ "About")

  * [Board Meeting Minutes](https://dced.pa.gov/cfa-board-meeting-minutes/)


### PIDA

  * [Board Meeting Minutes](https://dced.pa.gov/pida-board-meeting-minutes/ "Board Meeting Minutes")


### PMBDA
  * [About](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/business-financing/authorities/pennsylvania-minority-business-development-authority-pmbda/ "About")
  * [Board Members](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/business-financing/authorities/pennsylvania-minority-business-development-authority-pmbda/#PMBDA-board "Board Members")
  * [Board Meeting Minutes](https://dced.pa.gov/pmbda-board-meeting-minutes/)


### PEDFA
  * [About](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/private-financing/bond-financing/ "About")
  * [Board Members](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/private-financing/pedfa-board/ "Board Members")
  * [Board Meeting Minutes](https://dced.pa.gov/pedfa-board-meeting-minutes/)


### PCD Bank

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Business Financing
## The Office of Business Financing administers DCED’s loan, tax credit and grant programs, which provide financial assistance in order to stimulate job creation and growth for existing and new companies in PA
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") Business Financing
![Real Estate & Infrastructure](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/biz-finance.svg)
#### Business Financing
  * [Alternative and Clean Energy (ACE)](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/alternative-clean-energy-program-ace/ "Alternative and Clean Energy Program \(ACE\)")

  * [Business in Our Sites Loans (BOS)](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/business-in-our-sites-grants-and-loans-bos/ "Business in Our Sites Loans \(BOS\)")

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Keystone Opportunity Zones (KOZ)
## Tax Incentives and Development Opportunities Across Pennsylvania
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") Keystone Opportunity Zones (KOZs)
Pennsylvania is driving new waves of economic growth through its [Keystone Opportunity Zone (KOZ) Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/keystone-opportunity-zones-kozs/) − a program that represents a true partnership between state and local governments, school districts, economic development organizations, and community groups. KOZs have been designated across the Commonwealth to stimulate investment and job creation in underdeveloped or underutilized areas, and certain state and local tax benefits are available to qualified applicants in a designated KOZ parcel. Please see the [KOZ Program Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/koz-guidelines/?wpdmdl=88675) for all program requirements and specific tax benefits available.
##### Keystone Opportunity Zone Interactive Map
[The interactive KOZ map](https://experience.arcgis.com/experience/a0c46946201a482e96b91f61e9b397d9) provides an overview of the parcels within Pennsylvania that are currently designated as KOZs. Users may navigate to a pinned parcel to view basic information such as acreage, expiration date, and contact information for the regional KOZ coordinator. Please contact the KOZ coordinator for detailed information regarding specific parcels.
The map also features interactive tools along the left border that allow users to add additional layers to the map to see the proximity of various economic amenities to KOZ parcels, to filter and export amenities that are near a parcel, and to view relevant economic and demographic reports for the surrounding area. Navigating to the arrow at the bottom border allows users to view and download KOZ parcel data as a table.
[**VIEW KOZ MAP**](https://experience.arcgis.com/experience/a0c46946201a482e96b91f61e9b397d9)
**_PLEASE NOTE:_**_A desktop/laptop computer should be used for the best user experience with this web map. Persons with disabilities that will need assistance with the Pennsylvania Keystone Opportunity Zones Map should contact_ _ra-dcdced_gis@pa.gov_ _._
##### Tax Liability
Binding ordinances and resolutions are passed granting the waiver, abatement or exemption of certain state and local taxes. Depending on the situation, the tax burden may be reduced to zero through exemptions, deductions, abatements, and credits for the following:
  * State Taxes: Corporate Net Income Tax, Personal Income Tax, Sales and Use Tax, Bank Shares and Trust Company Shares Tax, Mutual Thrift Institutions Tax
  * Local Taxes: Earned Income/Net Profits Tax, Business Gross Receipts, Business Occupancy, Business Privilege and Mercantile Taxes, Local Real Property Tax, Sales and Use Tax


##### Length of Tax Relief
Zones expire at various times. Please check with the specific Zone Coordinator using the [interactive KOZ map](https://experience.arcgis.com/experience/a0c46946201a482e96b91f61e9b397d9) or view our [KOZ contacts list](https://dced.pa.gov/download/koz-coordinators-pdf/?wpdmdl=58226).
##### Administration
The Department of Community and Economic Development provides the organizational framework, including the certification and operations of KOZs. Local jurisdictions designate coordinators as a single point of contact for zone facilitation.
##### Application & Tools
  * [Keystone Opportunity Zone (KOZ) Online Application](https://apps.dced.pa.gov/koz/)
  * [KOZ Extension Application](https://dced.pa.gov/download/koz-extension-2014f-pdf/?wpdmdl=57674)
  * [KOZ Change of Status Notification](https://dced.pa.gov/koz-change-of-status/)
  * [KOZ Guidelines and Coordinators](https://dced.pa.gov/download/koz-guidelines/?wpdmdl=88675)

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.

# Foreign Direct Investment (FDI)
## A leader in cross-border projects, with the largest network of overseas offices of any U.S. state, Pennsylvania is committed to breaking down barriers for businesses looking to locate in the state
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") [International Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/international/ "International Assistance") Foreign Direct Investment (FDI)

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Strengthening Pennsylvania’s Military Communities
## The Pennsylvania Military Community Enhancement Commission (PMCEC) connects, advocates, and enhances the impact of PA’s military installations.
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") Pennsylvania Military Community Enhancement Commission (PMCEC)
The [Pennsylvania Military Community Enhancement Commission](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/pennsylvania-military-community-enhancement-commission/) ([PMCEC](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/pennsylvania-military-community-enhancement-commission/)) supports the Governor in strengthening the military presence and value within the Commonwealth. Its mission is to advocate for all military installations, organizations, and defense-related stakeholders in Pennsylvania.
The PMCEC is dedicated to promoting and enhancing Pennsylvania’s military community, ensuring the Commonwealth remains an excellent host for military organizations, service members, civilian personnel, and their families—both now and in the future.
##### Key Focus Areas
The PMCEC’s key focus areas address critical aspects of military support, from community engagement to workforce development, ensuring a strong and sustainable military presence in Pennsylvania.
  * Defense Community Engagement
  * Base Realignment and Closure (BRAC) Awareness
  * Economic Development Support
  * Workforce and Education Pipeline


![PMCEC Members](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/27845_DCED_PMCEC_Spring_Meeting_02-1024x819.jpg)
![Icon of Dollar Bills](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/svg/PaperMoney_BBlue.svg)
$47.75 Billion in Economic Impact (FFY 2023)
![Icon of New Jobs](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/svg/JobsRetain_BBlue.svg)
190,900 Pennsylvania jobs supported
![Icon of Bar Chart](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/svg/BarChart_BBlue.svg)
$21.8 billion in state spending (#7th)
![Icon of Pennsylvania](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/svg/Pennsylvania_BBlue.svg)
2.3% of PA’s GDP (#21st)

  * [PMCEC Installation Reference Manual](https://dced.pa.gov/download/pmcec-installation-reference-manual/?wpdmdl=62765)
  * [PA Military Installations Impact Reports](https://dced.pa.gov/library/?wpdmc2=pa-military-installation-impact-report,installation-reports)


##### Pennsylvania Military Installations
Pennsylvania Military Installations York Wyoming Wayne WestMoreland Washington Warren Venango Union Tioga Susquehanna Sullivan Somerset Snyder Schuylkill Potter Pike Philadelphia Perry Northumberland Northampton Montour Montgomery Monroe Mifflin Mercer McKean Lycoming Luzerne Lehigh Lebanon Lawrence Lancaster Lackawanna Juniata Jefferson Indiana Huntingdon Greene Fulton Franklin Forest Fayette Erie Elk Delaware Dauphin Cumberland Crawford Columbia Clinton Clearfield Clarion Chester Centre Carbon Cameron Cambria Butler Bucks Bradford Blair Berks Bedford Beaver Armstrong Allegheny Adams Tobyhanna Army Depot Biddle ANGB (111th Attack Wing / 270th EIS) Naval Support Activity Philadelphia Navy Yard Annex (Philadelphia Navy Yard) Fort Indiantown Gap (PA NG HQ & Training) Harrisburg ANG Base (193rd SOW) DLA Distribution Center – Susquehanna Naval Support Activity Mechanicsburg Carlisle Barracks (Army War College) Letterkenny Army Depot 316th Sustainment Cmd (Expeditionary) 171st AW 911th AW

Coraopolis, PA 15108

### PA Military Installations Information
####  911th AW 
##### Address
Brown St  
Coraopolis, PA 15108
[911th AW](https://www.pittsburgh.afrc.af.mil/)
### Pennsylvania Military Installations
Select a miliary installation from the dropdown to learn more information about each location.
Pennsylvania Military Installations Select Location Tobyhanna Army Depot Biddle ANGB (111th Attack Wing / 270th EIS) Naval Support Activity Philadelphia Navy Yard Annex (Philadelphia Navy Yard) Fort Indiantown Gap (PA NG HQ & Training) Harrisburg ANG Base (193rd SOW) DLA Distribution Center – Susquehanna Naval Support Activity Mechanicsburg Carlisle Barracks (Army War College) Letterkenny Army Depot 316th Sustainment Cmd (Expeditionary) 171st AW 911th AW
##### Questions?
Looking to learn more about PMCEC? Reach out to defense@pa.gov or 215.560.4404. Or fill out [our contact us form](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/pennsylvania-military-community-enhancement-commission-pmcec/pmcec-contact-form/) and let us know what we can assist you with.
[PMCEC Contact Form](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/pennsylvania-military-community-enhancement-commission-pmcec/pmcec-contact-form/)

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Center for Private Financing
## Assists Pennsylvania businesses in accessing financing by utilizing private sources of capital available through programs, including bond financing and loan guarantees
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") Center for Private Financing
> The Pennsylvania Economic Development Financing Authority (PEDFA) announces the following firms have been selected for the Pennsylvania Strategic Investments To Enhance Sites (PA SITES) Program bond issuance:
**Underwriters:**
  * BofA Securities, Inc.
  * Wells Fargo Corporate & Investment Banking
  * RBC Capital Markets
  * Siebert Williams Shank & Co. LLC


**Trustee:**
  * U.S. Bank Global Corporate Trust


![Bond Financing](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/money-bond.svg)
#### Bond Financing
  * [PEDFA Bond Financing Program](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/private-financing/bond-financing/ "PEDFA Bond Financing Program")
  * [Volume Cap Allocation](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/private-financing/volume-cap-allocation/ "Volume Cap Allocation")
  * [PEDFA Board](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/private-financing/pedfa-board/ "PEDFA Board")


![Loan Guarantees & Other Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/loan-guarantee.svg)
#### Loan Guarantees & Programs
  * [PA Capital Access Program](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/private-financing/pennsylvania-capitol-access-program/ "Pennsylvania Capital Access Program \(PennCAP\)")
  * [First Industries Guarantee Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/first-industries-fund-fif/ "First Industries Guarantee Program")
  * [TIF Guarantee Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/tax-increment-financing-tif-guarantee-program/ "TIF Guarantee Program")
  * [Second Stage Loan Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/second-stage-loan-program/ "Second Stage Loan Program")
  * [Business Opportunities Fund](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/business-opportunities-fund-bof/ "Business Opportunities Fund")
  * [Next Generation Farmer Loan Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/next-generation-farmer-loan-program/ "Next Generation Farmer Loan Program")
  * [PA Community Development Bank](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/pennsylvania-community-development-bank-loan-program/ "Pennsylvania Community Development Bank \(PCD Bank\)")
  * [New American Development Fund](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/pennsylvania-regional-center-new-american-development-fund/ "New American Development Fund")

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. # Workforce and Training
## A workforce ready to take your business to the next level
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") Workforce and Training
### Developing and Delivering Talent
Your ability to adapt to changes and meet opportunity head-on is dependent on the quality of your team. In Pennsylvania, you can be confident you’ll find the right fit. Our 6.4+ million-strong workforce brings experience, skill, curiosity, and a legendary can-do spirit to the table. Our nearly 300 higher-ed and post-secondary institutions produce talent sought out by companies around the world. Whether you’re looking for team members capable of supporting manufacturing operations, spearheading R&D and innovation, handling logistics and supply chain needs, or running your corporate headquarters, Pennsylvanians can get the job done. 
Pennsylvania is invested in bringing businesses and qualified workers together. We’ll help you find or develop your workforce, from apprentices to experienced experts and everyone in between. If you’re looking to upskill your employees or connect with leaders in your industry, we can help with our customizable and flexible workforce programs. 
Learn more about why Pennsylvania’s workforce will help your company succeed.
> _Data Disclaimer:_ The data on this page is updated twice a year, so may not be the latest available. Looking for the most current Pennsylvania data and statistics? We can help. Contact Debbie Tollett in the Strategic Information Services Office to get started with your inquiry.
### Explore the Data
### Workforce Development Brochure
Investing in training and retaining top talent is a priority for Pennsylvania. We have specialized technical and financial assistance to help your business develop the right workforce.
[ Download Brochure ](https://dced.pa.gov/download/workforce-development-brochure/?wpdmdl=105028 "Workforce Development Brochure")
### Local Employer Assistance
### PASmart
PASmart is a multi-pronged workforce initiative supporting students, jobseekers, and workers by connecting them with the information they need to succeed in our evolving economy. 
[ Learn More ](https://www.pasmart.pa.gov/ "PASmart")
### Digital Skills Training
Employers who would like to improve the digital skills of their employees can find free or low-cost trainings located near their business using the PA Digital Skills Training Map. The trainings can help employees with internet and computer skills to help them find information online, send or post information online, or better communicate using cell phones, apps, the internet, or other communication technology.
[ Learn More ](https://data.pa.gov/stories/s/bry2-xj2e "Digital Skills Training")
### Industrial Resource Centers (IRCs)
Part of the statewide Manufacturing PA initiative, the IRCs are a network of regional organizations focus on assisting manufacturers grow and stay competitive in a changing global economy. Workforce development consultations are part of their many available services. 
[ Learn More ](https://www.pamade.org/ "Industrial Resource Centers \(IRCs\)")
### Pennsylvania’s Industry Partnerships
Pennsylvania’s Industry Partnerships are regional industrial partnerships of businesses in a shared labor market who work with multiple teams to address overall competitiveness of the industry. Employers are welcome to join their local NGIP for networking, collaboration, and setting sustainable initiatives. 
[ Learn More ](https://www.dli.pa.gov/Businesses/Workforce-Development/Pages/Industry-Partnerships.aspx "Pennsylvania’s Industry Partnerships")
### WEDnetPA Workforce Grants
The Workforce & Economic Development Network of Pennsylvania (WEDnetPA) is a DCED-funded workforce training program helping employers upskill employees to fill in employment gaps. 
[ Learn More ](http://www.wednetpa.com/ "WEDnetPA Workforce Grants")
### Foundations in Industry Training (FIT) Grants
The DCED-funded Foundations in Industry Training (FIT) Grant Program can be used to help cover the costs of formal instruction or classroom requirements associated with apprenticeships. 
[ Learn More ](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/foundations-in-industry-training-fit-grant-program/ "Foundations in Industry Training \(FIT\) Grants")
### Manufacturing PA Training-to-Career Grant
Supporting new and innovative training focused on developing the skill sets of entry-level applicants in the manufacturing industry to better meet the needs of hiring employers. 
[ Learn More ](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/manufacturing-pa-training-career-grant-program/ "Manufacturing PA Training-to-Career Grant")
### PASmart
PASmart is a multi-pronged workforce initiative supporting students, jobseekers, and workers by connecting them with the information they need to succeed in our evolving economy. 
[ Learn More ](https://www.pasmart.pa.gov/ "PASmart")

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Keystone Innovation Zone (KIZ) Coordinator Locations
## There are 29 KIZ regions located in Pennsylvania. Contact your coordinator to find out if your business is eligible for the KIZ Tax Credit program
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Business Assistance](https://dced.pa.gov/business-assistance/ "Business Assistance") Keystone Innovation Zones (KIZ) Coordinator Locations
Keystone Innovation Zone Map MONTGOMERY BEAVER BUCKS CHESTER ERIE WASHINGTON FAYETTE CAMBRIA ALLEGHENY BERKS UNION MON- TOUR COLUMBIA NORTH- UMBERLAND DAUPHIN HUNTINGDON CENTRE BLAIR BEDFORD INDIANA LACKA- WANNA LUZERNE PHILADELPHIA LANCASTER WARREN CRAWFORD MERCER CLARION MONROE LEHIGH NORTHAMPTON ELK JEFFERSON CLEARFIELD GREENE WESTMORELAND LYCOMING YORK LAWRENCE BUTLER ARMSTRONG VENANGO FOREST SOMERSET McKEAN POTTER TIOGA BRADFORD SULLIVAN SUSQUEHANNA WYOMING WAYNE PIKE CAMERON CLINTON MIFFLIN SNYDER JUNIATA PERRY CUMBERLAND ADAMS FULTON FRANKLIN CARBON SCHUYKILL LEBANON
### Keystone Innovation Zone
Click on the regions or icons in Pennsylvania to see contact information about each Keystone Innovation Zone.
### KIZ Quick Facts
Keystone Innovation Zones | Inception to date | 2018 Calendar Year  
---|---|---  
Jobs created | 11,671 | 1,057  
Jobs retained | 31,000 | 2,957  
Businesses assisted | 7,120 | 56  
PA graduates hired | 3,555 | 273  
Patents filed | 2,957 | 138  
Patents awarded | 838 | 52  
Software copyrights filed | 292 | 75  
Software copyrights awarded | 127 | 29  
Licenses granted | 45,051 | 2,472  
Licensing revenue | $254,264,964 | $53,491,506  
RDT&E expenditures | $1,107,640,699 | $102,719,658  
Tax Credits Awarded (2006 to 2017) | $150,813,495 | $14,740,036

**Counties Represented** : York
##### KIZ Facts
  * As a component of the Rendell Administration’s Economic Stimulus Plan, the Keystone Innovation Zone (KIZ) program (2004) was designed to address the lack of entrepreneurial activity and knowledge economy growth around Pennsylvania’s research and development clusters.
  * These KIZs were established around institutions of higher education located in both rural and urban regions and focus on utilizing Pennsylvania’s institutions of higher education as economic drivers.
  * Each KIZ contains different partnerships from their region that include, but not limited to economic development providers, local governments, financial institutions and venture capitalists.
  * Each KIZ focuses on its region’s individual competitive target industries.
  * The 29 KIZs located throughout Pennsylvania serve both rural and urban regions and are partnered up with over 91 institutions of higher education (IHE) that range from research universities to local community colleges.
  * Since inception, the KIZ program has assisted in the creation of more than 11,600 jobs.


##### Funding
|   
---|---  
Keystone Innovation Zone (KIZ) | $17,480,525  
|   
**Keystone Innovation Zone Companion Programs** |   
Keystone Innovation Grant (KIG) Technology Commercialization | $9,864,427  
Keystone Innovation Zone Starter Kit (KISK) Faculty Recruitment | $11,500,229  
**TOTAL:** | $21,364,656

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Coal-Fired Power Plant Redevelopment Playbooks
## Pennsylvania Breathes New Life Into Decommissioned Coal-fired Power Plants Through An Assessment Of Redevelopment Options
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") Coal-Fired Power Plant Redevelopment Playbooks
Pennsylvania’s energy mix is changing. Some of our former coal-fired power plants have been converted to run on natural gas. Others are being retired and are now ready for a new use. These former power plant sites offer many opportunities for new business, with unparalleled river, road, rail, and energy transmission infrastructure.
DCED’s decommissioned coal-fired power plant redevelopment playbooks are a collaboration between state and local government, community and business stakeholders, environmental analysts, and market experts. Each customized playbook includes an assessment of the regional economy, overview of the site’s potential liabilities, and at least three reuse options.
[Learn More](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-funding/coal-plant-redevelopment-playbooks/mitchell-power-station/)

Disposal Area](https://dced.pa.gov/coal-fired-power-plant-redevelopment-playbooks-option-1/robena-coal-refuse-disposal-area/)

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120

[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/) Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
# Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
  * [Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/weatherization-assistance-program-guidelines/?wpdmdl=115898)
  * [Contact Us](https://dced.pa.gov/contact-us)
  * [](javascript:window.print\(\) "Print")


###### Overview
Pennsylvania’s Weatherization Assistance Program increases energy efficiency in homes by reducing energy costs and increasing comfort while safeguarding health and safety.
[Learn More](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/weatherization/)
###### Uses
On site energy audits are conducted to assess conditions in homes and to identify the most cost-effective energy saving measures to be installed. Weatherization services include but are not limited to:
  * Blower door guided air sealing to effectively locate and reduce air-leakage throughout the home
  * Installation of attic, wall, basement and crawlspace insulation and ventilation to reduce energy loss
  * Heating system modification or replacement to increase the efficiency and/or safety of the heating system
  * Minor repairs, and/or health and safety measures are provided (when necessary) to allow the safe and effective installation of the weatherization measures
  * Client education on the proper use and maintenance of the installed Weatherization measures and ways to reduce energy waste everyday


###### Eligibility
Low-income individuals (at or below 200% of the federal poverty level), with priority given to higher risk residents such as the elderly, disabled individuals, families with children and high energy users.
###### Funding
The average expenditure per household is $7,669 depending on the home audit results.
###### Terms
N/A
###### How to Apply
If you are an indvidual or household, find the [weatherization agency](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/weatherization/agency-list/) that serves your county.

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120

# Commonwealth Financing Authority (CFA)
## CFA Application Schedule
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs & Funding](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-funding/ "Programs & Funding") Commonwealth Financing Authority (CFA)
The Commonwealth Financing Authority (CFA) was established in 2004 as an independent agency to administer Pennsylvania’s economic stimulus packages. The CFA holds fiduciary responsibility over the funding of programs and investments in Pennsylvania’s economic growth. Unique among state agencies in structure and scope, the CFA consists of seven Board members: four legislative appointees and the secretaries of the Department of Community and Economic Development, the Office of the Budget and Department of Banking and Securities. Project approval requires five affirmative votes, four of which must come from legislative appointees.
Reference the [CFA Application Schedule](https://dced.pa.gov/download/?wpdmdl=68709) for a list of CFA program openings.
![Local Government Training](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/overview.svg)

  * [Interest Rates](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-funding/commonwealth-financing-authority-cfa/cfa-interest-rates/)

  * [Multimodal Transportation Fund](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/multimodal-transportation-fund/ "Multimodal Transportation Fund")
  * [Pipeline Investment Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/pipeline-investment-program/ "Pipeline Investment Program")

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120

[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/) Opportunity Scholarship Tax Credit Program (OSTC)
# Opportunity Scholarship Tax Credit Program (OSTC)
  * [Bus. Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/opportunity-scholarship-tax-credit-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84409)
  * [Org. Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/opportunity-scholarship-tax-credit-org-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84193)
  * [Apply](https://grants.pa.gov/)
  * [Contact Us](https://dced.pa.gov/contact-us)
  * [](javascript:window.print\(\) "Print")


The program provides tax credits to eligible businesses contributing to an Opportunity Scholarship Organization. Business contributions are then used by Opportunity Scholarship Organizations to provide tuition assistance in the form of scholarships to eligible students residing within the boundaries of a low-achieving school to attend another public school outside of their district or nonpublic school.
> **Upcoming School Year 25/26 EITC/OSTC Reporting Requirements:**
  * Identify whether a scholarship recipient was an eligible student with a disability. Along with the grade level of the recipient (K-8) or (9-12). 
  * Dollar amount of each individual scholarship award
  * Name of each scholarship recipients school district of residence. 
  * Name of the school that each recipient attended. 
  * Reporting begins with the 25/26 school year. Renewal reports due November 2026. Format to be provided by DCED.


> [2024-2025 OSTC Business Participants](https://dced.pa.gov/ostc-business-participants-by-county/)
###### Overview
**Students and Parents**
[How to apply for a scholarship](https://dced.pa.gov/opportunity-scholarship-tax-credit-program-faq/)
**Businesses**
[Application Guide for Businesses](https://dced.pa.gov/download/apply-single-application-short-app-121516/?wpdmdl=73144)
The program provides tax credits to eligible businesses contributing to an Opportunity Scholarship Organization. Business contributions are then used by Opportunity Scholarship Organizations to provide tuition assistance in the form of scholarships to eligible students residing within the boundaries of a low-achieving school to attend another public school outside of their district or nonpublic school. A low-achieving school is defined as a public elementary or secondary school ranking in the bottom 15 percent of their designation as an elementary or secondary school based upon combined math and reading Pennsylvania System of School Assessment (PSSA) scores.
[View the list of Opportunity Scholarship Organizations.](https://dced.pa.gov/opportunity-scholarship-organizations/)
###### Business Application Timeline
Tax credits to eligible businesses contributing to an Opportunity Scholarship Organization.
  * **May 15-June 30:** Business applicants who have fulfilled their 2-year commitment and wish to reapply in FY 25/26 to renew their 2-year commitment.
  * **May 15-June 30:** Businesses who are in the middle of their 2-year commitment.
  * **July 1:** All other businesses including initial applicants and those applicants wishing to submit an additional application on top of their previously submitted 2-year commitment.
  * If you did not submit the [tax clearance form](https://paebrprod.powerappsportals.us/EBR/DOR/Tax-Credit-Economic-Development-Clearance/) during the application process, you can still do so through the PA Department of Revenue website.


###### For Parents, Students and Opportunity Scholarship Organizations
Students who live within the attendance boundaries of a low-achieving school, as determined by the Pennsylvania Department of Education, are eligible to receive a scholarship if their household’s annual income is no greater than $116,055 plus $20,428 for each dependent member of the household. For the 2025-26 school year, the maximum scholarship award available to non-special education students is $8,500 and the maximum for a special education student is $15,000.
###### Uses
Tax credits may be applied against the tax liability of a business for the tax year in which the contribution was made. The tax credits awarded to businesses will be equal to 75 percent of their contribution amount, which can be increased to 90 percent upon the business committing for two years. The total may not exceed $750,000 per taxable year.
###### Eligibility
Businesses authorized to do business in Pennsylvania who are subject to one or more of the following taxes: 
  * Personal Income Tax
  * Capital Stock/Foreign Franchise Tax
  * Corporate Net Income Tax
  * Bank Shares Tax
  * Title Insurance & Trust Company Shares Tax
  * Insurance Premium Tax (excluding unauthorized, domestic/foreign marine)
  * Mutual Thrift Tax
  * Malt Beverage Tax
  * Surplus Lines Tax


###### Funding
Tax credits equal to 75 percent of its contribution up to a maximum of $750,000 per taxable year; can be increased to 90 percent of the contribution, if a business agrees to provide same amount for two consecutive tax years. Total program funding is $50 million.
###### Terms
An approved company must provide proof to DCED within 90 days of the notification letter that the contribution was made within 60 days of the notification letter; Tax credits not used in the tax year the contribution was made may not be carried forward or carried back and is not refundable or transferable, except for an approved election by a pass-through entity to apply any unused tax credits to the tax liability of the owners in the taxable year immediately following the year in which the contribution is made.
###### How to Apply
**Businesses**  
Pennsylvania businesses can begin applying for Opportunity Scholarship Tax Credits through the [Enterprise eGrants System](https://grants.pa.gov/). **DCED will no longer require applicants to mail the signed signature page.**
The [business application guide](https://dced.pa.gov/download/apply-single-application-short-app-121516/?wpdmdl=73144) explains the process of applying. Tax credit applications will be processed on a first-come, first-served basis by day submitted. All applications received on a specific day will be processed on a random basis before moving on to the next day’s applications. Applications will be approved until the amount of available tax credits is exhausted.
**Organizations**  
Applicants interested in applying as an Opportunity Scholarship Organization can apply at DCED Center for Business Financing, Tax Credit Division, 4th Floor, Commonwealth Keystone Building, 400 North Street, Harrisburg, PA 17120: (717)-787-7120 or RA-EITC@pa.gov[guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/opportunity-scholarship-tax-credit-org-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84193).
**Parents****/Students**  
The list of approved Opportunity Scholarship Organizations can be found on the [Parents/Student FAQ page](https://dced.pa.gov/programs-and-funding/opportunity-scholarship-tax-credit-program-faq/).
###### Additional Information
[Opportunity Scholarship Tax Credit Program (OSTC) Frequently Asked Questions](https://dced.pa.gov/eitc-ostc-frequently-asked-questions/)
**Finding Underperforming Schools**  
The Pennsylvania Department of Education is responsible for maintaining a list of the lowest 15 percent of underperforming schools within the commonwealth. This information can be accessed on the [Department of Education website](https://www.education.pa.gov/K-12/Opportunity%20Scholarship%20Tax%20Credit%20Program/Pages/default.aspx).
**List of Opportunity Scholarship Organizations**
[View the list of Opportunity Scholarship Organizations](https://dced.pa.gov/opportunity-scholarship-organizations/)
[Opportunity Scholarship Tax Credit – Economically Disadvantaged Schools](https://dced.pa.gov/download/opportunity-scholarship-tax-credit-economically-disadvantaged-schools-guidelines-2019/?wpdmdl=92251)
An Economically Disadvantaged School is defined as any school within this Commonwealth at which at least 75% of the students attending the school in the preceding school year received an Opportunity Scholarship tax credit award. The Department is authorized to approve only one Opportunity Scholarship Organization – Economically Disadvantaged Schools per year.

  * Community

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120

[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/) Community Development Block Grant (CDBG)
# Community Development Block Grant (CDBG)
  * [Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/cdbg-program-guidelines/?wpdmdl=65545)
  * [Apply](https://grants.pa.gov/)
  * [Contact Us](https://dced.pa.gov/contact-us)
  * [](javascript:window.print\(\) "Print")


###### Overview
Grants and technical assistance for eligible municipalities as identified under Pennsylvania Act 179 of 1984 as amended, for any eligible community development activities.
###### Uses
Housing rehabilitation, public services, community facilities, infrastructure improvement, development and planning.
###### Eligibility
Two components:
  * Entitlement program which provides annual funding to designated municipalities
  * Competitive program is available to all municipalities that are not direct federal recipients of CDBG funds and state Act 179 entitlement municipalities with a population less than 10,000


###### Funding
Entitlement funding is set by Act 179 formula. Competitive Program has a minimum request of $100,000 and has no ceiling limit. 
###### Terms
Seventy percent of each entitlement grant must be used for activities that benefit low- and moderate-income persons. Refer to program guidelines.
###### Additional Resources
  * [Community Development Block Grant Fact Sheet](https://dced.pa.gov/download/cdbg-factsheet/?wpdmdl=85894)


### Program Details
#### Program Categories
  * Grant


#### Funding Types
  * Community
  * Local Governments

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

### — Pennsylvania Stories —
[![Play YouTube Video In Popup](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/youtube-play-button-homepage.png)![PA Gets It Done](https://img.youtube.com/vi/GYV1FK4JNvc/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/embed/GYV1FK4JNvc?cc_load_policy=1&rel=0&autoplay=1)
#### PA Gets It Done
We welcome innovators, entrepreneurs, and anyone with a big idea to come see how Pennsylvania gets it done.
[![Play YouTube Video In Popup](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/youtube-play-button-homepage.png)![Erie's Story: AcousticSheep](https://img.youtube.com/vi/6rcATuBz4FY/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/embed/6rcATuBz4FY?cc_load_policy=1&rel=0&autoplay=1)
#### Erie's Story: AcousticSheep
Learn how Erie's location is helping companies grow and flourish with key resources and workforce collaboration.
[![Play YouTube Video In Popup](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/youtube-play-button-homepage.png)![Gilson Snow is Growing in PA](https://img.youtube.com/vi/zT_zHiOlYZU/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/embed/zT_zHiOlYZU?cc_load_policy=1&rel=0&autoplay=1)
#### Gilson Snow is Growing in PA
Gilson Snow makes custom snowboards and skis using Pennsylvania Poplar wood and ships their products to 50 countries around the world.
[![Play YouTube Video In Popup](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/youtube-play-button-homepage.png)![Start Your Own Business in PA](https://img.youtube.com/vi/w_w5_oj7OSI/maxresdefault.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/embed/w_w5_oj7OSI?cc_load_policy=1&rel=0&autoplay=1)
#### Start Your Own Business in PA
The Pennsylvania Business One-Stop Shop is a go-to resource for planning, registering, operating, and growing a business in PA.

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Consolidated Plan, Annual Plans & Reports
## The Consolidated Plan for Housing and Community Development is DCED’s application to the U.S. Department of Housing & Urban Development (HUD) to access federal funds for various community development programs
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Housing and Development](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/ "Housing and Development") Consolidated Plan, Annual Plans & Reports
The Department of Community and Economic Development is responsible for developing and executing The Consolidated Plan for Housing and Community Development (Consolidated Plan). The Consolidated Plan required by the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) (24CFR Section 91.115) is designed to help states and local jurisdictions to assess their affordable housing and community development needs and market conditions, and to make data-driven, place-based investment decisions.
The Consolidated Plan serves as a framework to identify housing and community development priorities that align and focus funding from HUD’s Community Planning and Development Programs:
  * [Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/community-development-block-grant-cdbg/)
  * [HOME Investment Partnerships (HOME) Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/home/)
  * [Emergency Solutions Grants (ESG) Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/emergency-solutions-grant-esg/)
  * [Disaster Recovery Resources](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/community-services/disaster-recovery/)
  * [Neighborhood Stabilization Program (NSP)](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/neighborhood-stabilization-program-nsp/)
  * [National Housing Trust Fund (NHTF)](https://www.phfa.org/legislation/act105.aspx), administered by the Pennsylvania Housing Finance Agency (PHFA)
  * [Housing Opportunities for Persons with Aids (HOPWA) Program](https://www.health.pa.gov/topics/programs/HIV/Pages/Services.aspx), administered by the PA Department of Health


The Consolidated Plan identifies the housing, community development, homelessness, public service, and economic development needs of the non-entitled municipalities of the Commonwealth. It establishes goals and objectives in addressing those needs and charts the course for expenditure of housing and community development funds over a period of five years in resolving the needs.
There are a few critical components of the Consolidated Plan, including:
  1. **Consultation and Citizen Participation.** Through the Consolidated Plan grantee jurisdictions engage the community, both in the process of developing and reviewing the proposed plan, and as partners and stakeholders in the implementation of Community Planning and Development (CPD) programs. By consulting and collaborating with other public and private entities, grantees can align and coordinate community development programs with a range of other plans, programs, and resources to achieve greater impact.
  2. **The Consolidated Plan.** The Consolidated Plan, which may have a duration of between 3 and 5 years, describes the jurisdiction’s community development priorities and multiyear goals based on an assessment of housing and community development needs, an analysis of housing and economic market conditions and available resources.
  3. **The Annual Action Plan.** The Consolidated Plan is carried out through Annual Action Plans, which provide a concise summary of the actions, activities, and the specific federal and non-federal resources that will be used each year to address the priority needs and specific goals identified by the Consolidated Plan. However, rather than describing specific projects to be funded, a State’s Annual Action Plan must specify its method of distributing funds to eligible units of local government and nonprofits, or the activities to be undertaken by the state-run programs in the coming year.


##### Annual Action Plan
Annually, the Commonwealth submits the Annual Action Plan to HUD detailing how each federal year’s allocation of funds will be utilized and the planned annual method to distribute funds.
##### Consolidated Annual Performance and Evaluation Report
At the conclusion of each program year, DCED reports to HUD the accomplishments and outcomes achieved as a result of the allocation of funds. The Consolidated Annual Performance and Evaluation Report (CAPER) details the use of funds, progress toward attaining the goals and individual and community beneficiaries.
##### Annual Action Plans and Reports
[Consolidated Plan – 2019-2023](https://dced.pa.gov/download/final-2019-2023-consolidated-plan-2019-annual-action-plan/?wpdmdl=113684) | [CDBG – Disaster Recovery](https://dced.pa.gov/disaster-recovery-library/)  
---|---  
[Action Plans – 2019-2023](https://dced.pa.gov/library/?wpdmc2=action-plans-2019-2023) | [CAPERs – 2019-2023](https://dced.pa.gov/library/?wpdmc2=capers-2019-2023/)  
[Citizen Participation Plan](https://dced.pa.gov/library/?wpdmc2=citizen-participation-plan/) | [Action Plans – 2024-2028](https://dced.pa.gov/library/?wpdmc2=action-plans-2024-2028/)  
[Consolidated Plan – 2024-2028](https://dced.pa.gov/download/consolidated-plan-2024-2028/?wpdmdl=121993) | [CAPERs – 2024-2028](https://dced.pa.gov/library/?wpdmc2=capers-2024-2028/)

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.

Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120

# Act 47 Financial Distress
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Local Government Services](https://dced.pa.gov/local-government/ "Local Government Services") Act 47 Financial Distress
The Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) administers the [Municipalities Financial Recovery Act, Act of 1987, P.L. 246, No. 47](http://www.legis.state.pa.us/WU01/LI/LI/US/HTM/1987/0/0047..HTM). Under Act 47, DCED has a responsibility to assist Pennsylvania municipalities that are experiencing severe financial difficulties in order to ensure the health, safety and welfare of their citizens.
Click on the counties with Act 47 Determinations to view available documents on those cities, towns and boroughs.
ACT 47 MAP Newville Chester Harrisburg

### DISTRESS DETERMINATION RESCISSIONS
A timeline of municipalities that have recovered from financial distress under Act 47
  * Borough of Shenandoah, Schuylkill County - Designated 5/20/88; Rescinded 4/16/93
  * Borough of Ambridge, Beaver County - Designated 4/10/90; Rescinded 4/16/93
  * Borough of Wilkinsburg Allegheny County - Designated 1/19/88; Rescinded 11/10/98
  * Borough of East Pittsburgh - Allegheny County Designated 11/13/92; Rescinded 12/27/99
  * Borough of North Braddock – Allegheny County Designated 5/22/95; Rescinded 4/11/03
  * Borough of Homestead, Allegheny County – Designated 3/22/93; Rescinded 3/28/07

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.

Harrisburg
