[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/) Educational Improvement Tax Credit Program (EITC)
# Educational Improvement Tax Credit Program (EITC)
  * [Bus. Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/educational-improvement-tax-credit-program-eitc-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84406)
  * [SO & EIO Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/eitc-organization-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84187)
  * [Pre-K Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/eitc-pre-k-organization-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84183)
  * [Apply](https://grants.pa.gov/Login.aspx)
  * [Contact Us](https://dced.pa.gov/contact-us)
  * [](javascript:window.print\(\) "Print")


> **Upcoming School Year 25/26 EITC/OSTC Reporting Requirements:**
  * Identify whether a scholarship recipient was an eligible student with a disability. Along with the grade level of the recipient (K-8) or (9-12). 
  * Dollar amount of each individual scholarship award
  * Name of each scholarship recipients school district of residence. 
  * Name of the school that each recipient attended. 
  * Reporting begins with the 25/26 school year. Renewal reports due November 2026. Format to be provided by DCED.


> [2024-2025 EITC Business Participants](https://dced.pa.gov/eitc-business-participants-by-county/)
###### Overview
Tax credits to eligible businesses contributing to a Scholarship Organization, an Educational Improvement Organization, and/or a Pre-Kindergarten Scholarship Organization.
View a list of Approved Organizations:   
[Scholarship Organizations (SO)](https://dced.pa.gov/scholarship-organizations/)   
[Educational Improvement Organizations (EIO)](https://dced.pa.gov/educational-improvement-organizations/)   
[Pre-Kindergarten Scholarship Organizations (PKSO)](https://dced.pa.gov/pre-kindergarten-scholarship-organizations/)
###### Business Application Timeline
Tax credits to eligible businesses contributing to Scholarship Organization, an Educational Improvement Organization, and/or a Pre-Kindergarten Scholarship Organization. 
  * **May 15-June 30:** Business applicants who have fulfilled their 2-year commitment and wish to reapply in FY 25/26 to renew their 2-year commitment.
  * **May 15-June 30:** Businesses who are in the middle of their 2-year commitment.
  * **July 1:** All other businesses including initial applicants and those applicants wishing to submit an additional application on top of their previously submitted 2-year commitment.
  * If you did not submit the [tax clearance form](https://paebrprod.powerappsportals.us/EBR/DOR/Tax-Credit-Economic-Development-Clearance/) during the application process, you can still do so through the PA Department of Revenue website.


Pass-through entities, such as S-Corporations, Partnerships, LLCs, etc., can now apply the same day as C-Corporations. Applications must be submitted electronically using the Enterprise eGrants System. See the business application guide in the box above.  

###### For Parents, Students and Scholarship Organizations and/or Pre-K Scholarship Organizations 
Students are eligible to receive a scholarship if their household’s annual income is no greater than $116,055 plus $20,428 for each dependent member of the household.
  

###### OSTC
[Learn about the Opportunity Scholarship Tax Credit Program (OSTC)](https://dced.pa.gov/ostc)
[Business Application Guide](https://dced.pa.gov/download/apply-single-application-short-app-121516/?wpdmdl=73144)
###### Uses
Tax credits may be applied against the tax liability of a business for the tax year in which the contribution was made.
###### Eligibility
Businesses authorized to do business in Pennsylvania who are subject to one or more of the following taxes:
  * Personal Income Tax
  * Capital Stock/Foreign Franchise Tax
  * Corporate Net Income Tax
  * Bank Shares Tax
  * Title Insurance & Trust Company Shares Tax
  * Insurance Premium Tax (excluding unauthorized, domestic/foreign marine)
  * Mutual Thrift Tax
  * Malt Beverage Tax
  * Surplus Lines Tax


###### Funding
Tax credits equal to 75 percent of its contribution up to a maximum of $750,000 per taxable year. Can be increased to 90 percent of the contribution, if business agrees to provide same amount for two consecutive tax years. For contributions to Pre-Kindergarten Scholarship Organizations, a business may receive a tax credit equal to 100 percent of the first $10,000 contributed and up to 90 percent of the remaining amount contributed up to a maximum credit of $200,000 annually.
###### Terms
An approved company must provide proof to DCED within 90 days of the notification letter that the contribution was made within 60 days of the notification letter. Tax credits not used in the tax year the contribution was made may not be carried forward or carried back and are not refundable or transferable, except for an approved election by a pass-through entity to apply any unused tax credits to the tax liability of the owners in the taxable year immediately following the year in which the contribution is made.
###### How to Apply
**Businesses**
Pennsylvania businesses can begin applying for EITC credits through the Enterprise eGrants System. **DCED will no longer require applicants to mail the signed signature page**.
The [business application guide](https://dced.pa.gov/download/apply-single-application-short-app-121516/?wpdmdl=73144) explains the process of applying. Tax credit applications will be processed on a first-come, first-served basis by day submitted. All applications received on a specific day will be processed on a random basis before moving on to the next day’s applications. Applications will be approved until the amount of available tax credits is exhausted.
**Organizations**
Applicants interested in applying as an Educational Improvement Organization, Scholarship Organization or Pre-K Organization can apply at DCED Center for Business Financing, Tax Credit Division, 4th Floor, Commonwealth Keystone Building, 400 North Street, Harrisburg, PA 17120: 717.787.7120 or RA-EITC@pa.gov
###### Additional Information
[Educational Improvement Tax Credit Program (EITC) Frequently Asked Questions](https://dced.pa.gov/eitc-ostc-frequently-asked-questions/)

  * [Pre-Kindergarten Scholarship Organization](https://dced.pa.gov/download/eitc-pre-k-organization-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84183)

  * [Educational Improvement Organizations](https://dced.pa.gov/educational-improvement-organizations/)
  * [Pre-Kindergarten Scholarship Organizations](https://dced.pa.gov/pre-kindergarten-scholarship-organizations/)

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Programs and Funding
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") Programs and Funding
## Search for Funding
[Archived Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/archived-programs/)
  * [All](https://dced.pa.gov/program/)
  * [Business](https://dced.pa.gov/program/)
  * [Community](https://dced.pa.gov/program/)
  * [Local Government](https://dced.pa.gov/program/)


  * [ All ](https://dced.pa.gov/program/)
  * [Grant](https://dced.pa.gov/program/ "A grant is a non-repayable sum of money awarded for a specified purpose. We have a variety of grant programs available, from those that fund research and development projects and police regionalization to those that assist startup businesses and flood control projects.")
  * [Loan](https://dced.pa.gov/program/ "A loan is a lent sum of money that is paid back with interest. You can find loans here for funding projects in distressed communities, jump-starting small businesses and more.")
  * [Loan Guarantee](https://dced.pa.gov/program/ "A loan guarantee is a promise by a lender to assume the debt obligations of a borrower if he or she defaults on a loan.")
  * [Tax Incentives](https://dced.pa.gov/program/ "A listing of program tax credits \(sums of money awarded to offset specific taxes\) and tax abatements \(subsidies that lower the cost of owning real and personal property by reducing or eliminating the taxes a company pays on it\).")
  * [Bonds](https://dced.pa.gov/program/ "A bond, also known as a fixed-income security, is a debt instrument created for the purpose of raisingcapital. They are essentially loan agreements between the bond issuer and an investor, in which the bond issuer is obligated to pay a specified amount of money at specified future dates.")


Program Search Go to Program
Step 1 |  **Search:** Use the criteria above to filter through programs such as funding, loans, tax credits and other incentives.  
---|---  
Step 2 |  **Download and Read the Guidelines:** Read the program's guidelines thoroughly to see if your project fits the requirements  
Step 3 |  **Apply Through The Enterprise eGrants System:** If after reading the guidelines you feel your project fits the guidelines, register and apply using the [Enterprise eGrants System](https://grants.pa.gov/Login.aspx). Applying does not guarantee acceptance into the programs.  
### [Abandoned Mine Drainage Abatement and Treatment Program (AMDATP)](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/abandoned-mine-drainage-abatement-treatment-program-amdatp/ "Abandoned Mine Drainage Abatement and Treatment Program \(AMDATP\)")
Act 13 of 2012 establishes the Marcellus Legacy Fund and allocates funds to the Commonwealth Financing Authority (the “Authority”) for abandoned mine drainage, abatement, and treatment. Abandoned Mine Drainage (AMD) is one of the largest sources of stream impairment in Pennsylvania. Billions of gallons of AMD impair over 5,500 miles of streams within the Commonwealth.

Overview Article Act 84 of 2016 (Article XVII-D, the Entertainment Production Tax Credit) authorizes the issuances of Video Game Production Tax Credit to expand the video gaming production in Pennsylvania. […] 
April 4, 2023 136 
### [Waterfront Development Tax Credit Program (WDTC)](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/waterfront-development-tax-credit-program-wdtc/ "Waterfront Development Tax Credit Program \(WDTC\)")
Overview Program provides tax credits to business firms who provide contributions to waterfront development organizations who are undertaking a waterfront development project. The program encourages private investment in waterfront property […] 
October 16, 2025 137 
### [Watershed Restoration and Protection Program (WRPP)](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/watershed-restoration-protection-program-wrpp/ "Watershed Restoration and Protection Program \(WRPP\)")
Overview Act 13 of 2012 establishes the Marcellus Legacy Fund and allocates funds to the Commonwealth Financing Authority for watershed restoration and protection projects. The overall goal of the Watershed […] 
February 5, 2026 138 
### [Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/weatherization-assistance-program-wap/ "Weatherization Assistance Program \(WAP\)")
Overview Pennsylvania’s Weatherization Assistance Program increases energy efficiency in homes by reducing energy costs and increasing comfort while safeguarding health and safety. Learn More Uses On site energy audits are […] 
December 4, 2025 139 
### [WEDnetPA](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/wednetpa/ "WEDnetPA")
Created by the Department of Community & Economic Development and made available through the Workforce and Economic Development Network of Pennsylvania – WEDnetPA – the program provides qualified employers training funds for new and existing employees. 
November 18, 2025 140

Show Navigation Menu [CONTACT US](https://pagetsitdone.com/contact_us)
Why Pennsylvania?
# Pennsylvania Works **Smarter**
![Governor Josh Shapiro with Amazon Employees holding the ribbon during inauguration](https://s7d9.scene7.com/is/image/statepa/23952_gov_amazon_dz_016-copy-1?fmt=webp)
##  The Fuel for **Future-Forward Thinking**
### **So, why PA?** Maybe a better question is: why go anywhere else?
Pennsylvania delivers an experienced workforce, world class higher education institutions, and a prime East Coast location near four of the nation’s 10 largest markets. All that, plus an exceptional quality of life. Here, innovation thrives with vibrant communities and a dynamic business ecosystem dedicated to getting it done.
Home to the innovative and ingenious, Pennsylvania is the most economically diverse state in the nation. We’re a powerful energy producer, leader in academic research, and hub of agriculture and advanced manufacturing. And we’re ready to fuel business success. Our business-friendly tax climate is designed for growth — we’re on track to cut the corporate net income tax rate down to 4.99% by 2031, making it among the lowest in the nation. Combine that with nationally ranked universities fostering top talent and a robust transportation infrastructure that connects companies to the world, and you’ll see why Pennsylvania is a place where big ideas grow — and so can businesses. Home to 20 Fortune 500 companies, state-of-the-art R&D facilities, and more than one million small businesses, Pennsylvania is where a rich history meets your promising future.
## Navigation Cards
### [Location & Infrastructure ](https://pagetsitdone.com/why-pa/location_and_infrastructure)

### [Key Industries ](https://pagetsitdone.com/key_industries)
### [Success Stories ](https://pagetsitdone.com/why-pa/success_stories)
## Pennsylvania
### A prime East Coast location with
### an **exceptional** quality of life
##  #7 Best State to Start a Business 
[ Forbes Advisor, 2024 (opens in a new tab)](https://www.forbes.com/advisor/business/best-states-to-start-a-business/#:~:text=Pennsylvania%20lands%20at%20number%20four,national%20average%20at%20%2427.7%20million)
#1 Best Business School (University of Pennsylvania, Wharton) [ U.S. News & World Report, 2025 (opens in a new tab)](https://www.usnews.com/best-graduate-schools/top-business-schools/mba-rankings)
20 Number of Fortune 500 Companies Located in Pennsylvania  [ Visual Capitalist, 2024 (opens in a new tab)](https://www.visualcapitalist.com/map-the-number-of-fortune-500-companies-in-each-u-s-state/)
6.5 Million Strong Workforce [ U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, 2023 (opens in a new tab)](https://www.bls.gov/eag/eag.pa.htm)
26th Largest Economy in the World [ World Bank Group, 2024 (opens in a new tab)](https://datacatalog.worldbank.org/search/dataset/0038130)
![](https://pagetsitdone.com/content/dam/copa-pagid/newsletterpalogo.svg)

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.

[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/) COVID 19 – County Relief Block Grant Program
# COVID 19 – County Relief Block Grant Program
  * [Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/covid-19-county-relief-block-grant-guidelines/?wpdmdl=94990)
  * [Contact Us](https://dced.pa.gov/contact-us)
  * [](javascript:window.print\(\) "Print")


### Overview
Under the provisions of the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act, 2020 Enacted H.R. 748, 116 Enacted H.R. 748, the “CARES Act”), the U.S. Department of the Treasury is authorized to transfer funds to the Commonwealth to cover costs of the Commonwealth that: are necessary expenditures incurred due to the public health emergency with respect to the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID-19); “were not accounted for in the budget most recently approved as of the date of enactment of this section for the State or government; and “were incurred during the period that begins on March 1, 2020, and ends on December 30, 2020. 
### Uses
  * Offsetting the cost of direct county response, planning & outreach efforts related to COVID-19.
  * Small Business Grant Programs to support businesses with fewer than 100 employees & to support businesses and other entities that are primarily engaged in the tourism industry.
  * Grant programs to support the following entities for costs related to assisting businesses during the COVID-19 Disaster Emergency: CEDOs, LDDs, IRCs, SBDCs, EDCs.
  * Assistance to cities, boroughs, incorporated towns or townships located within eligible counties for response and planning efforts related to COVID-19.
  * Behavioral Health & Substance use disorder treatment services.
  * Nonprofit assistance programs for entities that are an exempt organizations under section 501(C)(3) OR 501(C)(19) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.
  * Broadband internet deployment with priority given to unserved or underserved areas.


### Eligibility
Only county governments, those not directly funded by the Federal Government, are eligible to receive the COVID-19 County Block Grant Program funding through the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED).
Note: There are 60 out of 67 PA counties that meet this criteria.
### Funding
  * Minimum Distribution – No county shall receive a distribution of less than $1,000,000. 
  * Proration – The distribution to a county shall be adjusted on a pro rata basis to the extent necessary to meet the minimum distribution requirements and not to exceed the total amount appropriated for the COVID-19 County Relief Block Grant. 
  * Exclusion – A county with a population that is greater than 500,000 that has received a disbursement directly from the Federal Government through the CARES Act, is not eligible to receive funds. 
  * Population Proportion Amount – The population proportion amount shall be determined as follows:
    * The allocation of funding distributed to each county is based on a formula which takes into consideration the total of each county’s population as a percentage of the total population of the sixty counties (6,905,235) not receiving direct assistance via the CARES Act minus those counties receiving the minimum allocation of $1,000,000. That percentage is then used to determine the allocation for each county of the $625,000,000.
    * Population estimate is defined as the County’s population shall be equal to the published estimate by the United States Census Bureau Population estimates for Calendar Year 2019.


### Terms
Eligible expenditures must have been incurred during the period that began on March 1, 2020 and ending on December 30, 2020.
### Additional Information
[County Relief Block Grant Financial Status Report](https://dced.pa.gov/download/county-relief-block-grant-financial-status-report/?wpdmdl=95619)
[County Relief Block Grant Guidance Webinar ](https://register.gotowebinar.com/recording/3991175940499861263)  
Pennsylvania counties will learn about the use of funds, contracting and monitoring procedures, and what is an eligible activity or expenditure for the COVID-19 County Relief Block Program.
[County Relief Block Grant FAQs (11.02.2020)](https://dced.pa.gov/download/county-cares-faq-updated-8-18-2020/?wpdmdl=103134)
[COVID-19 County Relief Block Grant Presentation ](https://dced.pa.gov/download/county-relief-qa-presentation-final-08-04-2020/?wpdmdl=95828)
  
County Relief Block Grant Program, Covid 19 County Relief, Covid-19 County Relief

  * Grant

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.

Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania

[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/) Neighborhood Assistance Program, Enterprise Zone Tax Credit (NAP/EZP)
# Neighborhood Assistance Program, Enterprise Zone Tax Credit (NAP/EZP)
  * [Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/neighborhood-assistance-program-enterprise-zone-tax-credit-nap-ezp-guidelines/?wpdmdl=86207)
  * [Apply](https://grants.pa.gov/Login.aspx)
  * [Contact Us](https://dced.pa.gov/contact-us)
  * [](javascript:window.print\(\) "Print")


###### Overview
An incentive program that provides tax credits to businesses or private companies investing in rehabilitating, expanding, or improving buildings or land located within designated enterprise zones.
###### Uses
Real property improvements such as rehab, expansion or physical improvements to buildings or land resulting in jobs created or retained.
View the [Neighborhood Assistance Program Enterprise Zone Tax Credit application checklist](https://dced.pa.gov/download/neighborhood-assistance-program-enterprise-zone-tax-credit-application-checklist/?wpdmdl=119442) to help guide you in your application submission.
###### Eligibility
Any private company with an investment located in an enterprise zone.
###### Funding
Tax credits equal 25% of amount invested; Up to $500,000 total tax credits per project.
###### Terms
Credits received and not used may be carried forward for up to five years. Credits may be sold or transferred. Please refer to the EZP Guidelines.
###### Additional Information
  * [Application to Sell or Assign Tax Credits Under the Neighborhood Assistance Act](https://dced.pa.gov/download/application-for-tax-credit-under-the-neighborhood-assistance-act/?wpdmdl=119447)
  * [Application for Tax Credit Under the Neighborhood Assistance Act](https://dced.pa.gov/download/application-for-tax-credit-under-the-neighborhood-assistance-act-2/?wpdmdl=119448)
  * [Form of Tax Credit Certificate](https://dced.pa.gov/download/form-of-tax-credit-certificate/?wpdmdl=119449)
  * [NAP/EZP Coordinators](https://dced.pa.gov/download/?wpdmdl=68662)
  * [Designated Distressed Municipalities in PA](https://dced.pa.gov/download/listing-of-eligible-distressed-areas/?wpdmdl=58237)


### Program Details
#### Program Categories
  * Tax Incentives


#### Funding Types
  * Business
  * Community

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.

Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/) Neighborhood Assistance Program (NAP)
# Neighborhood Assistance Program (NAP)
  * [Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/neighborhood-assistance-program-nap-guidelines/?wpdmdl=86209)
  * [Apply](https://grants.pa.gov/Login.aspx)
  * [Contact Us](https://dced.pa.gov/contact-us)
  * [](javascript:window.print\(\) "Print")


> [View the 2025-2026 Neighborhood Assistance Program approved projects.](https://dced.pa.gov/library/?wpdmc2=nap-2025-2026/%20)
###### Overview
Tax credit program to encourage businesses to invest in projects which improve distressed areas.
###### Uses
**Neighborhood Assistance Program**
A project must serve distressed areas or support neighborhood conservation. Projects must fall under one of the following categories: affordable housing programs, community services, crime prevention, education, job training or neighborhood assistance. A tax credit of up to 65% can be awarded.
View the [Neighborhood Assistance Program application checklist](https://dced.pa.gov/download/checklist-for-neighborhood-assistance-program/?wpdmdl=113831) to help guide you in your application submission.
**Neighborhood Partnership Program (NPP)**
Long-term collaborations (five years or more) of business, government, and community leaders to produce a comprehensive, asset-based and relationship-driven approach to community development. A tax credit of up to 90% or 95% can be awarded based on the length of the program (Please refer to the guidelines for more information).
View the [Neighborhood Partnership Program application checklist](https://dced.pa.gov/download/checklist-for-neighborhood-partnership-program/?wpdmdl=113833) to help guide you in your application submission.
**Special Program Priorities (SPP)**
This program targets on specific problems and projects which the state has designated as priorities (see NAP/SPP guidelines for a list of the priorities) in a distressed area. The tax benefit to a business firm contributing to an approved SPP application would be eligible for up to a 90% tax credit.
View the [Special Program Priorities application checklist](https://dced.pa.gov/download/checklist-for-special-program-priorities/?wpdmdl=113835) to help guide you in your application submission.
**Charitable Food Program (CFP)**
The Charitable Food Program (CFP) component of NAP is focused on improving food security in Pennsylvania. CFP assists charitable programs that provide food to low income populations in distressed areas. A tax credit of up to 65% can be awarded.
View the [Charitable Food Program application checklist](https://dced.pa.gov/download/checklist-for-charitable-food-program-nap-2022/?wpdmdl=113829) to help guide you in your application submission.
###### Eligibility
  * Nonprofit Neighborhood Organization
  * Businesses are only eligible for EZP


###### Funding
Tax credit percentage varies.
###### Terms
Refer to the guidelines.
###### Additional Information
  * [Neighborhood Assistance Program Impact](https://dced.pa.gov/download/neighborhood-assistance-program-impact/?wpdmdl=84815) ([also available in español](https://dced.pa.gov/download/neighborhood-assistance-program-impact-spanish/?wpdmdl=125741)) 
  * [NAP Business Fact Sheet](https://dced.pa.gov/download/nap-business-factsheet/?wpdmdl=124776) ([also available in español](https://dced.pa.gov/download/nap-business-fact-sheet-spanish/?wpdmdl=125742)) 
  * [NAP Business Contributor Process ](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/community-services/neighborhood-assistance-program-business-contributor/)
  * [NAP Business Contributor Handbook](https://dced.pa.gov/download/nap-business-contributor-handbook/?wpdmdl=114159)

Looking for the Enterprise Zone Program (EZP)?
Tax credit applicable to private companies which make qualified investments to promote community economic development in impoverished areas that have been designated as state Enterprise Zones.
[Learn more about the EZP component of NAP](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/neighborhood-assistance-enterprise-zone-tax-credit-napezp/)

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Programs](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/) COVID-19 PA Hazard Pay Grant
# COVID-19 PA Hazard Pay Grant
  * [Guidelines](https://dced.pa.gov/download/covid-19-pa-hazard-pay-grant-guidelines/?wpdmdl=95560)
  * [Contact Us](https://dced.pa.gov/contact-us)
  * [](javascript:window.print\(\) "Print")


> [COVID-19 PA Hazard Grant Awardees](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2020/08/COVID-19-PA-Hazard-Pay-Grant-Program_Funding_List_20200814.pdf)
### Overview
The COVID-19 PA Hazard Pay Grant Program was established to help employers provide hazard pay to employees in life-sustaining occupations during the coronavirus (COVID-19) pandemic. Hazard pay is intended to keep front-line employees working in sectors that are vital to every Pennsylvanians’ existence. The program will help employers provide hazard pay for their employees to retain current employees. This reimbursement-based grant is for employers who will be offering hazard pay over the eligible program period.
For general questions about the program or specific application questions, visit our [Hazard Pay FAQs](https://dced.pa.gov/covid-19-pa-hazard-pay-grant-program-frequently-asked-questions/).
### Eligibility
Businesses, healthcare non-profits, public transportation agencies, and certified economic development organizations may apply for funding for employers located and operating in Pennsylvania within one of the following eligible industries:
  * Healthcare and Social Assistance
  * Food Manufacturing
  * Food Retail Facilities
  * Transit and Ground Passenger Transportation
  * Security Services for eligible industries listed above and commercial industries that were not closed as a result of the Governor’s Business Closure Order 
  * Janitorial Services to Buildings and Dwellings


Additional details about eligible industries and employers can be found in the program guidelines.
### Funding
Grant funds may be used for hazard pay (as defined below) for direct, full-time and part-time employees earning less than $20/hour, excluding fringe benefits and overtime. Funds may only be used to pay hazard pay for eligible employees for the 10-week period from August 16, 2020 through October 24, 2020. Hazard pay means additional pay for performing hazardous duty or work involving physical hardship, in each case that is related to COVID-19.
Applicants may apply for up to $1,200 per eligible full-time equivalent (FTE) employee. The hazard pay must be paid to the eligible employee over the 10-week period of August 16, 2020 through October 24, 2020 as a $3/hour hazard pay increase to their regular pay rate. Employers may apply for a grant to provide hazard pay for up to 500 eligible full-time equivalent employees per location ($600,000 maximum grant per location). No Employer may receive more than $3,000,000 of the funding under the COVID-19 Hazard Pay Grant Program.
### How to Apply
**Applications will be received between July 16, 2020 and July 31, 2020**. To apply for funding, applications must be submitted through the [Enterprise eGrants System](https://grants.pa.gov/login.aspx) . For any questions, contact DCED Customer Service by calling 1.866.466.3972 or by email at ra-dcedcs@pa.gov.
### Additional Information
[Hazard Pay Frequently Asked Questions](https://dced.pa.gov/covid-19-pa-hazard-pay-grant-program-frequently-asked-questions/)
### Program Details
#### Program Categories
  * Grant


#### Funding Types
  * Business

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# About Us
## Pennsylvania Department of Community and Economic Development
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") About Us
> The Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) works every day to ensure Pennsylvania is a premier state to do business—driving innovation across a diverse mix of industry clusters, cultivating a resilient economy, and investing in our people and communities. For a quick, on-the-go snapshot of our mission, who we work with, and our key areas of focus across the Commonwealth, [download the DCED Fact Sheet](https://dced.pa.gov/download/pa-department-of-community-economic-development-fact-sheet/?wpdmdl=128337). Together, we are building a stronger Pennsylvania that works for all the residents, workers, businesses, and entrepreneurs that call our Commonwealth home.
![About Us](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/icons/pages/businessmen2.svg)

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120

--- SOURCE: https://dced.pa.gov/cedo ---

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States  

  * [About Us](https://dced.pa.gov/about-us/)

# Certified Economic Development Organizations
## Listed below are Certified Economic Development Organizations (CEDOs) organized by county
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") Certified Economic Development Organizations (CEDO)
A network of nonprofit economic development organizations, [known as CEDOs](https://dced.pa.gov/download/certification-of-economic-development-organizations-cedo-guidelines-2015/?wpdmdl=56161), partner with the Department of Community and Economic Development to administer the Pennsylvania Industrial Development Authority (PIDA) loan programs. If interested in submitting a PIDA loan application, please contact a Certified Economic Development Organization (CEDO) that serves the county your business is located in. The CEDO will discuss with you in detail how the application process works and will assist your business with packaging the loan application. For additional information about the PIDA loan program, please visit the [PIDA page](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/pennsylvania-industrial-development-authority-pida/).
### CEDO County List
Choose a Pennsylvania County to see contact information about each economic development organization within that county.

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Contact Us
## Having trouble finding something? Ask us and we'll help you. If we can't answer the question, we'll find the person that can
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") Contact Us
1.866.466.3972 
###### PA Business One-Stop Shop
1.833.722.6778

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# EITC & OSTC Frequently Asked Questions
## Learn more about the top asked questions for the Educational Improvement and Opportunity Scholarship Tax Credit Programs
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") EITC & OSTC Frequently Asked Questions
EITC & OSTC Frequently Asked Questions
What items need to be included as part of my EITC/OSTC renewal application?
  * [Scholarship Organizations](https://dced.pa.gov/download/eitc-organization-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84187)
  *     * Appendix III
    * Appendix IV
    * Compilation, Review or Audit for Organization’s FY18 year-end
    * Federal Form 990 or Proof of Exemption for Organization’s FY18 year-end
  * [Educational Improvement Organizations](https://dced.pa.gov/download/eitc-organization-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84187)
  *     * Appendix III
    * Appendix V
    * Updated Letters of Support which must be signed by District Superintendent 
    * Compilation, Review or Audit for Organization’s FY18 year-end
    * Federal Form 990 or Proof of Exemption for Organization’s FY18 year-end 
  * [Pre-K Scholarship Organizations](https://dced.pa.gov/download/eitc-organization-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84183)
  *     * Appendix VII
    * Appendix IX
    * Updated Curriculum Alignment Letters. See sample alignment letter as part of the Pre-K guidelines (Appendix X)
    * Compilation, Review or Audit for Organization’s FY18 year-end 
    * Federal Form 990 or Proof of Exemption for Organization’s FY18 year-end 
  * [Opportunity Scholarship Organizations](https://dced.pa.gov/download/eitc-organization-guidelines/?wpdmdl=84193)
  *     * Appendix XIII
    * Appendix XIV
    * Appendix XV
    * Compilation, Review or Audit for Organization’s FY18 year-end
    * Federal Form 990 or Proof of Exemption for Organization’s FY18 year-end


How do I complete my organizations monitoring report for this year?
All participating EITC/OSTC organizations operating on a fiscal year-end of 5/31 through 10/31 will be required to submit two Monitoring Reports – Part I as part of their FY 19/20 renewal application. For example, an organization with a 6/30 year-end will provide one Part I – Fiscal Report for FY end 6/30/18 and a second report for FY end 6/30/19 and likewise for organizations operating on similar fiscal year-ends. Organizations impacted by this change will only need to submit one Part II – Performance Report to be completed for the 2018-19 school year.
Should all business donations be included as part of my EITC/OSTC renewal documents?
No, participating non-profit organizations shall only report on direct tax credit donations from EITC/OSTC approved businesses. All other donations outside of tax credit participation including donations from other participating non-profits/private schools shall not be included as part of your renewal reports.
Do I need to submit an annual EITC/OSTC renewal application to remain eligible?
Yes, renewal documents are required to be submitted by all participating non-profit organizations annually on or after November 1st to maintain program eligibility.
How do I properly complete the Appendix V Part II – Performance Report?
Participants are only required to report on innovative educational programs previously approved by DCED. Do not include program expenditures unrelated to EITC.
Is it important to have access to previously submitted EITC/OSTC renewal applications?
Yes, it will significantly benefit those completing an updated renewal application to have access to prior submissions. Not only will you be able to see reported EITC/OSTC dollar amounts, but it will also assist participating EIO’s to know the format used for Performance Report submissions. 
Who do I contact if I am not familiar with the prior year’s renewal application?
Applicants can contact the Tax Credit Division at 717.787.7120 to be connected to their assigned project analyst.
How do I properly complete the OSTC Appendix XV Annual Report?
  * Question #1 & Question #2 = 185% of Federal Poverty Level  
Answers vary depending upon the size of the household
  * Question #3 & Question #4 = First Class School District   
School District of Philadelphia 
  * Question #5 & Question #6 = Financial Recovery School District   
Chester-Upland S.D., Duquesne City S.D., Harrisburg S.D., Penn Hills S.D., Scranton S.D., City of York S.D.

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Pennsylvania Housing Advisory Committee Meeting – March 11, 2026
  * STARTMar 11th - 10:00am
  * VENUE4th Floor, Commonwealth Keystone Building


## March 11 @ 10:00 am EDT
### Event Navigation
  * [« PIDA Board Meeting – March 4, 2026](https://dced.pa.gov/event/pida-board-meeting-march-4-2026/)
  * [PIDA Board Meeting – April 1, 2026 »](https://dced.pa.gov/event/pida-board-meeting-april-1-2026/)


**In Person Location:**  

Please notify Angela Susten, at 717.787.5327 or asusten@pa.gov, at least forty-eight hours prior to the public hearing to make the necessary arrangements, held at:   
**Commonwealth Keystone Building**  
Conference Room 4 West  
400 North Street, 4th Floor  
Harrisburg, PA 17120-0225 
**Microsoft Teams**  
Join on your computer, mobile app or room device  
[Click here to join the meeting](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_ODc4YTViM2QtOTdlNS00NjFkLWFkMDUtZWQwZGM5M2MxNzFh%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%22418e2841-0128-4dd5-9b6c-47fc5a9a1bde%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%22838bbaba-4ded-444a-9785-f4073645bd23%22%7d)
**Meeting ID** : 265 879 024 448 91   
**Passcode** : qK9zV7aJ 
[Download Teams](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-teams/download-app) | [Join on the web](https://www.microsoft.com/microsoft-teams/join-a-meeting)
**Dial by your location** : [+1 267 332 8737 ](tel:+1%20267%20332%208737) US (Philadelphia)   
**Phone Conference ID** : 461 458 568# 
[Find a local number](https://dialin.teams.microsoft.com/783683fb-6d69-4e8b-aa52-814e500ad7b8?id=208816849)
**Agenda**
During the meeting, DCED, DOH and PHFA will provide updates on their programs, the 2024 Consolidated Annual Performance and Evaluation Report (CAPER) will be reviewed, a summary of the needs reported on during the six Regional Housing Advisory Committee meetings will be shared, and there will be a discussion on the needs in communities in the areas of community development, affordable housing, and homelessness.
Add to calendar

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Center for Community & Housing Development
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Housing and Development](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/ "Housing and Development") Center for Community & Housing Development
The Pennsylvania Department of Community and Economic Development Center for Community and Housing Development is responsible for the administration of funding from the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) Community Planning and Development Programs. These include:
#### [Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/community-development-block-grant-cdbg/)
CDBG funds strengthen Pennsylvania communities by assisting with housing improvements, building suitable living environments, expanding economic opportunities geared to low- and moderate-income individuals, and improving critical community health and welfare infrastructure. To learn more about [the federal CDBG program, visit the HUD Exchange website](https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/cdbg/).

#### [HOME Investment Partnerships (HOME) Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/home/)
HOME provides municipalities with grants and technical assistance to expand the supply of affordable housing for low and very-low income Pennsylvanians. To learn more about [the federal HOME program, visit the HUD Exchange website](https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/home/).

#### [HOME American Rescue Plan (HOME-ARP)](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/home/)
HOME-ARP provides funding to assist individuals or households who are homeless, at risk of homelessness, or other vulnerable populations by providing housing, rental assistance, supportive services and non-congregate shelter, to reduce homelessness and increase housing stability.  
For more information [HOME-ARP Program | HUD.gov / U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)](https://www.hud.gov/).

#### [Emergency Solutions Grants (ESG) Program](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/emergency-solutions-grant-esg/)
ESG supports local efforts to help individuals and families that are currently experiencing homelessness, as well as those at risk. To learn more about [the federal ESG program, visit the HUD Exchange website](https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/esg/).

#### [Community Development Block Grant Disaster Recovery (CDBG-DR)](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/community-services/disaster-recovery/)
CDBG-DR provides funding for recovery support in federally designated disaster areas impacted by domestic major disasters and emergencies. To learn more about [the federal CDBG-DR program, visit the HUD Exchange website](https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/cdbg-dr/).

#### [Recovery Housing Program (RHP)](https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/rhp/)
RHP provides funding for stable, transitional housing for individuals in recovery from a substance-use disorder. To learn more about [the federal RHP program, visit the HUD Exchange website](https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/rhp/).
#### [Section 108](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/section-108-program/)
Section 108 enables states and local governments participating in the Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) program to obtain federally guaranteed loans to fund large economic development projects and undertake revitalization activities. To learn more about [the federal Section 108 program, visit the HUD Exchange website](https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/section-108/).
#### [National Housing Trust Fund (NHTF)](https://www.phfa.org/legislation/act105.aspx)
The NHTF is administered by the Pennsylvania Housing Finance Agency (PHFA) and provides grants to states in order to produce and preserve affordable housing for extremely low- and very low-income households. To learn more about [the federal NHTF program, visit the HUD Exchange website](https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/htf/).
#### [Housing Opportunities for Persons with Aids (HOPWA) Program](https://www.health.pa.gov/topics/programs/HIV/Pages/Services.aspx)
HOPWA is a program administered by the PA Department of Health and works to address the housing needs of low-income people living with HIV/AIDS and their families. To learn more about [the federal HOPWA program, visit the HUD Exchange website](https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/hopwa/).

##### Consolidated Plan
The Department of Community and Economic Development is responsible for developing and executing The Consolidated Plan for Housing and Community Development (Consolidated Plan). The Consolidated Plan required by the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) (24CFR Section 91.115) is designed to help states and local jurisdictions to assess their affordable housing and community development needs and market conditions, and to make data-driven, place-based investment decisions.
The Consolidated Plan serves as a framework to identify housing and community development priorities that align and focus funding from HUD's Community Planning and Development Programs. For more information on the Consolidated Plan, the PA Housing Advisory Committee, and/or the Annual Action Plans and Reports for HUD funded programs administered by DCED, please visit the [Consolidated Plan, Annual Plans & Reports page](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/consolidated-plan-annual-plans-reports/).

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.

Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania

# Disaster Recovery in PA
## When a natural disaster strikes, affected communities can coordinate with DCED to begin the recovery process
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Housing and Development](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/ "Housing and Development") [Community Services](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/community-services/ "Community Services") Disaster Recovery
The Department of Community & Economic Development (DCED) provides access to many disaster recovery resources for individuals, local government officials, and communities that enable them to rebuild, enact preventative measures, and remain resilient after a natural disaster strikes. Learn more about available grants, federal programs, and insurance resources that are available for those who have experienced the effects of a natural disaster.
##### DCED Disaster Recovery Programs and Resources
###### [Community Development Block Grant – Disaster Recovery (CDBG-DR)](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/community-development-block-grant-disaster-recovery-cdbg-dr/)
The PA Department of Community & Economic Development administers U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) awards of Community Development Block Grant Disaster Recovery funding, which provide recovery support in federally designated disaster areas impacted by domestic major disasters and emergencies.
Visit our [CDBG-DR library](https://dced.pa.gov/disaster-recovery-library/) to view action plans and resources related to disaster recovery in Pennsylvania.
###### **Hurricane Ida Disaster Recovery**
Pennsylvania received approximately $38 million to address needs related to the recovery from Hurricane Ida in 2021. Learn more about this funding on the [Hurricane Ida Disaster Recovery webpage](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/community-services/disaster-recovery/hurricane-ida-disaster-recovery-in-pa/).
###### **Tropical Storm Lee and Hurricane Irene Disaster Recovery**
DCED has allocated approximately $57 million in CDBG-DR funding to address remaining needs related to Tropical Storm Lee and Hurricane Irene in 2011. At this time, no grant applications for funding are being accepted.
###### **Tropical Storm Debby Disaster Recovery**
Pennsylvania received approximately $14 million to address needs related to the recovery from Tropical Storm Debby. Learn more about this funding and the [Tropical Storm Debby Action Plan – August 2025](https://dced.pa.gov/download/cdbg-disaster-recovery-p-l-118-158-admin-action-plan/?wpdmdl=126226) ([Spanish](https://dced.pa.gov/download/cdbg-disaster-recovery-p-l-118-158-admin-action-plan-spanish/?wpdmdl=127524)).
##### State & Federal Disaster Recovery Resources
The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) also offers resources for the National Disaster Recovery Framework and programs and to see a complete list, visit the [FEMA website](http://www.fema.gov/media-library/assets/documents/117794). Individuals looking for assistance with recovery claim issues should visit the [Pennsylvania Emergency Management website](https://www.pema.pa.gov/responseandrecovery/Disaster-Assistance/pages/individual_assistance.aspx).
  * [Pennsylvania Emergency Management Agency – Hazard Mitigation](http://www.pema.pa.gov/responseandrecovery/Disaster-Assistance/Pages/Mitigation.aspx)
  * [Pennsylvania Emergency Management Agency – Public Assistance](http://www.pema.pa.gov/responseandrecovery/Disaster-Assistance/Pages/Public-Assistance-Program.aspx)
  * [Pennsylvania Emergency Management Agency – Individual Assistance](http://www.pema.pa.gov/responseandrecovery/Disaster-Assistance/Pages/Individual_Assistance.aspx)
  * [Pennsylvania Emergency Management Agency – Flood Assistance & Insurance](https://www.pema.pa.gov/Floodplain-Management/Pages/default.aspx?web=1&wdLOR=c7D1F0B88-6210-4763-9E82-F90819AE6006)
  * [Federal Emergency Management Agency – Region III](http://www.fema.gov/region-iii-dc-de-md-pa-va-wv)


##### Contact Us
For more information about Disaster Recovery Resources in Pennsylvania, email us at ra-dccdbg-dr@pa.gov. For those interested in obtaining a report or document in a different language, we offer document translation services. You may request this service by emailing us and specifying the document and in what language you would like it translated.

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.

Harrisburg

# Homelessness in PA
## Homeless Information in Pennsylvania
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Housing and Development](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/ "Housing and Development") Homelessness in PA
Homelessness continues to be an issue impacting many communities across the Commonwealth – a problem that is not just an urban issue but also can be found in even the most rural counties. On any given day, over 12,000 Pennsylvanians are known to be homeless across the Commonwealth of PA.
Across the nation, Continuums of Care (CoC) planning bodies are charged with promoting a community-wide commitment to the goal of ending homelessness; to provide funding for efforts by nonprofit providers, states, Indian Tribes or tribally designated housing entities and local governments to quickly rehouse homeless individuals, families, persons fleeing domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking, and youth while minimizing the trauma and dislocation caused by homelessness; to promote access to and effective utilization of mainstream programs by homeless individuals and families, and to optimize self-sufficiency among those experiencing homelessness.
In Pennsylvania, there are 16 different Continuum of Care planning bodies currently organized to end homelessness in their covered regions. Two of these CoCs are known as the Balance of State and cover 20 counties in Western PA and 33 counties in Central and Eastern PA which are known to be the most rural counties in the Commonwealth. The remaining 14 CoCs are single county planning operations which cover urban communities. Each CoC have their own policies and procedures, resources, and systems to ending homeless.
### Point-in-Time Count
The Point-in-Time (PIT) Count is a count of sheltered and unsheltered people experiencing homelessness on a single night in January. HUD requires that CoCs conduct an annual count of people experiencing homelessness who are sheltered in emergency shelter, transitional housing, and Safe Havens on a single night. CoCs also must conduct a count of unsheltered people experiencing homelessness every other year. Each count is planned, coordinated, and carried out locally. This data is essential for HUD on tracking trends of homelessness from year to year which is reported to congress in the [Annual Homeless Advisory Report (AHAR)](https://www.huduser.gov/portal/datasets/ahar.html) and helps inform funding allocations at the national level. You can view the most recently published [PIT data aggregated for the Commonwealth of PA or by individual CoC](https://www.hudexchange.info/resource/3031/pit-and-hic-data-since-2007/) on the HUD Exchange website.

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# HOME
## The HOME program provides funds to develop and maintain affordable housing, using target, leverage and impact criteria as its priorities
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Housing and Development](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/ "Housing and Development") [Individuals and Households](https://dced.pa.gov/?page_id=1176 "Individuals and Households") HOME
These grants frequently leverage other governmental, non-profit or private funds to supplement the HOME funds for a greater impact.
Grants provided in support of housing for special-needs populations, including the mentally and physically disabled, demonstrates those target criteria.
#####  Program Details
[Learn more](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/home) about the details of the program and how to have your community apply for the program.

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Modular - Industrialized Housing
## Modular and Industrialized Housing in PA
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Housing and Development](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/ "Housing and Development") Modular – Industrialized Housing
> **The Pennsylvania Industrialized Housing & Buildings Program will [adopt the 2021 Codes](https://www.pacodeandbulletin.gov/Display/pabull?file=/secure/pabulletin/data/vol55/55-48/1602.html) effective Monday, June 1, 2026**. This means, all industrialized housing and buildings entering the first stage of production on June 1, 2026, must be constructed in accordance with these codes.
> Facilitating the manufacture of factory constructed housing and buildings through efficient and effective regulatory oversight of uniform standards established for the mutual benefit of the public, industry and local municipalities throughout the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania.
**[Modular housing](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/commercial-modular-buildings/)** , which is primarily designed for residential use, is wholly or mostly made, constructed, fabricated or assembled in manufacturing facilities. These structures are then shipped to the home site for assembly and installation. The modular home does not include a structure or building classified as an institutional building or manufactured home.
A modular home is different from a **[manufactured house](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/manufactured-housing/)** as it is designed only for installation on a site built permanent foundation and not to be moved after the installation. Generally, modular homes comply with the same building code as conventional, site constructed homes.
![Pennsylvania Insignia for Industrialized Housing](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/mh_insignia1_ylw.png)Pennsylvania Insignia for Industrialized Housing
![Pennsylvania Insignia for Industrialized Housing Component](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/mh_insignia1_blue.png)Pennsylvania Insignia for Industrialized Housing Component
Every **[modular home](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/commercial-modular-buildings/)** and component constructed for placement in the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania must be certified and bear the Pennsylvania Insignia of Certification (shown above), as well as the insignia of the inspection agency that evaluates and approves the designs, facilities, construction techniques, materials and quality assurance program used in each facility.
Although [modular homes](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/commercial-modular-buildings/) are built to comply with the model building codes and are exempt from the PA UCC, building officials make important contributions to the effective administration of the program. In addition to their oversight in terms of site preparation, foundation construction and utility connections, building code officials shall assist the Department by conducting site inspections of modular housing and components prior to installation for apparent damage occurring during transportation and other apparent non-conformity.
##### Approved Manufacturers
DCED has issued the Pennsylvania Notice of Approval to each of the [listed manufacturers of industrialized housing, housing components, industrialized buildings or building components](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/website-list-of-manufacturers/) that indicates the approval of the manufacturer’s building systems documentation, compliance assurance program and the authority to receive and attach insignias of certification.
This list identifies the limitations of each manufacturers approval, (residential, commercial or both) as noted on the Notice of Approval.
Manufacturers who would like approval to construct modular homes for placement in Pennsylvania, please email Michael Moglia, Chief, Housing & Buildings Standards Division, at mmoglia@pa.gov.
###### **Third-Party Agencies for Evaluations and Inspections**
Current Third-Party Agencies under currently contract with DCED to perform evaluations and inspections of industrialized housing (residential) and housing components: 
**(For those agencies who would like to become a contracted third-party agency with PA DCED, please email; Michael Moglia, Chief, Housing & Buildings Standards Division at mmoglia@pa.gov)**

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120

# Insurance
## Protecting Pennsylvania Municipalities and Local Government Officials Against Liability
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Housing and Development](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/ "Housing and Development") Insurance
One of the greatest responsibilities of local government officials is protecting their municipality against liability. This requires an understanding of the vulnerabilities to loss as well as the prevention practices that will help control costs.
##### Risk Management Publications
The Governor’s Center for Local Government Services has developed a [Risk Manager’s Insurance Guide](https://dced.pa.gov/download/risk-managers-insurance-guide-2019/?wpdmdl=92471) to provide guidance in loss control practices for local officials. This publication contains information on the extent of municipal liability, types of municipal insurance, public risk management and how to purchase insurance coverage. The Center also provides training and technical assistance on risk management.
##### Fire Insurance
[Fire Insurance Escrow List](https://dced.pa.gov/local-government/fire-emergency-services/fire-insurance-escrow-list) – Of particular importance to insurance companies, the escrow list shows the registration of municipalities with Act 93 of 1994.
##### National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP)
As of September 7, 2021, DCED no longer houses or has responsibility for any aspect of the National Floodplain Insurance Program (NFIP). Legislation fully transferred the program to the Pennsylvania Emergency Management Agency (PEMA) and Emily Houdeshell will serve as the Pennsylvania NFIP Coordinator. For more information or questions, visit the [Pennsylvania Floodplain Management and Flood Insurance webpage](https://www.pema.pa.gov/Floodplain-Management/Pages/default.aspx?web=1&wdLOR=c7D1F0B88-6210-4763-9E82-F90819AE6006) on the PEMA website.

[ ![PA Department of Community & Economic Development](https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/DCED-Hori_2025.png) ](https://dced.pa.gov) https://dced.pa.gov/wp-content/themes/business2015/images/logo-l.png
PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
400 North Street  
Harrisburg  
Pennsylvania  
17120  
United States

# Main Street Matters
## Main Street Matters is a program that assists Pennsylvania’s core communities in achieving revitalzation
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Housing and Development](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/ "Housing and Development") Main Street Matters
The Main Street Matters program supports physical improvements to both designated and other communities that are undertaking revitalization to restore deteriorated downtowns and residential neighborhoods. The program also provides funding for accessible modifications for the homes of persons with physical disabilities.
##### The Two Program Designations
###### Main Street
Funding and technical assistance for a community’s downtown or commercial district.
###### Elm Street
Funding and technical assistance for residential areas and neighborhoods adjacent to the community’s downtown or commercial district.
##### Program Details
[Learn more](https://dced.pa.gov/programs/main-street-matters/) about the details of the program and how to have your community apply for the program.
###### SHARE
[](javascript:%20void\(0\) "Share Page on Facebook")[](javascript:%20void\(0\) "Share Page on X")[](javascript:%20void\(0\) "Share Page on LinkedIn")

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.

# Manufactured Home Installer FAQ
## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) for Manufactured Home Installer
[DCED](https://dced.pa.gov "PA Department of Community & Economic Development") [Housing and Development](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/ "Housing and Development") Manufactured Home Installer FAQ
What must I do to become a HUD Licensed Manufactured Home Installer?
Complete a [HUD approved training course](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/manufactured-housing#Maryland).
Will my HUD Manufactured Home Installers License be recognized in other states?
Yes, as long as the other state accepts the HUD License.
As a retailer, what are my responsibilities under the HUD Installation Program?
Complete the [HUD Form 305](https://www.hud.gov/sites/documents/305.PDF) & [HUD Form 306](https://www.hud.gov/sites/documents/306.PDF) and forward to HUD. In PA this will take effect on 10/1/2020.
Are there different rules or requirements for installing a new manufactured home in a community or other rented lots?
No. New manufactured homes are required to be installed consistent with the manufacturer’s approved installation instructions, without exception.
What are the requirements for the installation of used manufactured homes?
[Specifics when relocating a pre-owned manufactured home.](https://dced.pa.gov/housing-and-development/manufactured-housing#relocating-preowned)
Can a home be installed in a manner not specifically addressed by the manufacturer?
No. The manufacturer must provide instructions for the installation. Designs prepared by professional engineers or registered architects may not be utilized in place of the manufacturer’s instructions.
What about floating slabs?
All foundations must be protected from frost heave, therefore a floating slab foundation is unacceptable.
Can an addition or deck be attached to a home?
Not without approval from the home manufacturer. Typically, all additions, decks, etc. must be free-standing from the home and constructed in accordance with local building codes.

PA Department of Community and Economic Development
The mission of the Department of Community and Economic Development (DCED) is to foster opportunities for businesses to grow and for communities to succeed and thrive in a global economy. Our mission is to improve the quality of life for Pennsylvania citizens while assuring transparency and accountability in the expenditure of public funds.
